races.race_name: Swinton Park Silver Anniversary Cup Handicap

runners.name: I Can Boogy
runners.spotlight: Has run well in classifieds on AW this year, winning such an event at Newcastle (1m) in February, but has not been as good in a couple of handicaps; well handicapped on her best turf form.
runners.comment: Has ran well in classifieds on AW this year, winning one at Newcastle in February.

runners.name: Far Ahead
runners.spotlight: Has failed to deliver in handicaps since landing a Catterick novice in May 2025; his mark continues to plummet but he needs to show more.
runners.comment: Has failed to deliver in handicaps since landing a Catterick novice in May 2025.
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Daring Leader
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with victory at Newcastle (1m2f) in September but he's yet to fire this spring, only eighth in 9.5f Wolverhampton handicap last month; needs to take a big step forward despite a slipping mark.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with victory at Newcastle in September but he's yet to fire this spring.
runners.owner: D J Jones & M T Brougham

runners.name: Barleybrown
runners.spotlight: Won four times on Newcastle AW in 2024 but winless since; needs to be held up off a strong pace but not really firing at present.
runners.comment: Won four times on Newcastle AW in 2024 but winless since.

runners.name: Quirke On Parole
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm) last June; last of ten back there (8.5f) on reappearance last month but should strip fitter here and not out of this.
runners.comment: Got off the mark at Beverley last June but last of ten back there last month.
runners.owner: A Quirke & Mrs G B Walford

runners.name: Oilisa
runners.spotlight: Landed a brace at Ayr and Nottingham around 1m last October on heavy; still lightly raced and should be better for the run when tenth of 22 in amateurs' handicap at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) in March; one to keep an eye on.
runners.comment: Landed a brace at Ayr and Nottingham around 1m last October on heavy.

runners.name: Haaf A Diamond
runners.spotlight: 1-23 but ran well on her reappearance when third of ten at Nottingham (8.5f, good) last month in first-time cheekpieces (retained since); not so good when sixth of 11 at Southwell (1m, AW) since but holds claims if she can run to her best.
runners.comment: 1-23 but ran well on her reappearance when third of ten at Nottingham last month.
runners.owner: Dr M Voikhansky & Mrs S Voikhanskaya

runners.name: Penny Ghent
runners.spotlight: 26-race maiden; two poor efforts on turf this year; easy to dismiss.
runners.comment: 26-race maiden; two poor efforts on turf this year; easy to dismiss.

runners.name: Ring Of Gold
runners.spotlight: All five wins over 7f on AW; 0-21 on turf and doesn't appeal for win purposes despite career-low mark.
runners.comment: 0-21 on turf and has dropped to a career-low mark; doesn't appeal for win purposes.

runners.name: Balqaa
runners.spotlight: All five wins have came on AW; usually ridden with hold-up tactics and will need plenty of luck in running to get her head in front here.
runners.comment: Usually ridden with hold-up tactics and will need plenty of luck in running.

runners.name: Arranmore
runners.spotlight: Last of four wins on turf came in August 2021 but he's been in good order on AW recently, winning at Southwell (1m3f) in March; not sure that the drop back in trip will suit on this return to turf.
runners.comment: Last of four wins on turf came in August 2021 but won on Southwell AW in March.

runners.name: Woodleigh
runners.spotlight: Scored off this mark at Redcar (1m2f, good to soft) last October; kept on well on his last two starts at Beverley over 8.5f suggesting that a step back up in trip may be beneficial.
runners.comment: Kept on well on his last two starts at Beverley over 8.5f and a step up in trip may suit.

runners.name: Volenti
runners.spotlight: Has four wins on Newcastle AW but is only 1-19 on turf; never figured on return to turf when seventh of nine at Pontefract (1m, good) in cheekpieces (left off here) last month; needs to show more.
runners.comment: Has four wins on Newcastle AW but is only 1-19 on turf.

runners.name: Little Ted
runners.spotlight: Has three course wins over 1m2f; hasn't figured at Redcar or Ayr since returning to turf last month; best watched.
runners.comment: Has three course wins over 1m2f; hasn't figured since returning to turf last month.

races.race_name: Mark Leather's 70th Birthday Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Peggy Boo
runners.spotlight: Signs of ability in three 2yo runs but she has finished last in her two sprint handicap runs this year (6f, AW and 5f, good); dropping down the weights but she has a point to prove.
runners.comment: Finished last in two handicap runs this spring; down in weights but with a point to prove
runners.owner: S Ullyott, T Coyle & P Robinson

runners.name: Pivotal Terms
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at the ninth time of asking when leading late in a 6f handicap at Newcastle five weeks ago; inconvenienced by dropping to 5f at Musselburgh next time but ran well enough to think she was still in form; not handicapped out of things.
runners.comment: 6f AW win last month before finding 5f too sharp latest; still in form; each-way shout

runners.name: Simba's Pride
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in maiden/novice company at Thirsk (5f to 1m) and he duly stepped forward on his handicap debut at Hamilton two weeks ago (6f, good), always prominent and proving too strong for the reopposing Ellie's De Vega; attracted market support on that occasion (14-1 into 15-2) and he looks a major player up 3lb.
runners.comment: Well backed when making a winning h'cap debut at Hamilton two weeks ago; big player up 3lb

runners.name: Ellie's de Vega
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; two solid third-place finishes in 6f handicaps this spring, shaping second best behind Simba's Pride at Hamilton a fortnight ago (bit short of room and lost second close home); 3lb turnaround in the weights may be insufficient.
runners.comment: Front-runner; two solid efforts in 6f h'caps this year; closely matched with Simba's Pride

runners.name: Tenor's Inn
runners.spotlight: Ran well at Beverley (5f, good to firm) last August, beaten under one length into fourth in a nursery off an 8lb higher mark than today's; no progress since and he needs to leave his reappearance effort well behind him.
runners.comment: Chance on his Beverley fourth last August but he has been well beaten in three runs since

runners.name: Gunalt Wavelength
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden but she has finished second on four occasions, including her seasonal return four weeks ago (6f, good to firm; handles soft); runs off the same mark and holds each-way claims once again.
runners.comment: 0-10 but four times a runner-up, including on seasonal return; in the mix once more

runners.name: Vega's Virtue
runners.spotlight: No immediate improvement for the switch to handicaps when seventh of nine, beaten just over 4l, in a 7f event at Doncaster a fortnight ago; still has time to do better but Hares Bredth may prove the stable's main hope.
runners.comment: No improvement for handicapping when 7th at Doncaster (7f) 15 days ago; yard runs two here

runners.name: Hares Bredth
runners.spotlight: Ran his best race to date when keeping on for third in a 0-55 handicap at Redcar ten days ago (6f, good), racing further away from the stands' rail than the pair to beat him; still has some potential in minor sprint handicaps and he is more appealing than many.
runners.comment: Not ideally positioned when third of 14 at Redcar ten days ago; still capable of better

runners.name: What A Tahoo
runners.spotlight: She has run to a modest level in her six starts on AW, showing best form when fourth at Newcastle in February (6f); hopes rest on the switch to turf giving her a lift.
runners.comment: Modest form in six AW runs; needs improvement for the switch to turf
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

races.race_name: Mark Leather's 70th Birthday Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Carnation Queen
runners.spotlight: Won a York seller last summer (6f, good to firm); handicaps have proved more demanding and she was well short of her best at Southwell 17 days ago; headgear now reached for.
runners.comment: Won a seller last June but handicaps have proved more challenging; now tried in headgear

runners.name: Maynora
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 11th attempt when always prominent at Pontefract early last month (6f, good to soft); a 4lb rise and stall 1 could be his downfall this time.
runners.comment: 11th-time lucky when scoring at Pontefract last month; 4lb higher and drawn widest today

runners.name: I'm Dan Dare
runners.spotlight: He took a good step forward on his second handicap run ten days ago (6f, good; cheekpieces first time), always prominent and only picked off close home; up 2lb but not fully exposed.
runners.comment: Just touched off at Redcar ten days ago; a 2lb rise shouldn't prevent another big run

runners.name: Call Me By My Name
runners.spotlight: Early promise for James Horton last season but his campaign rather fizzled out; dropped away in the final 300yds at Haydock on his seasonal/stable debut three weeks ago but he may have needed the run and that was a 0-70; should last longer today.
runners.comment: Drops back in class with his seasonal/stable debut under his belt; can do better today

runners.name: Battenburg Belle
runners.spotlight: Ran well for third at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) on her turf and handicap debut in April; not built on it in two 1m runs since but could still have potential as a sprinter.
runners.comment: Promising 3rd on handicap/turf debut; twice beaten over 1m since; unexposed as a sprinter

runners.name: Dance Hello
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who finished out the back on her seasonal debut at Wolverhampton in March (6f; made the running); nothing so far to suggest she is well handicapped.
runners.comment: Modest maiden; little evidence to suggest she is the answer today

runners.name: Solar Cooper
runners.spotlight: Ran well on his 2yo debut but little to celebrate since; slowly edging down the weights and now tried in headgear but others look safer.
runners.comment: Still to build on the promise of his debut; connections now reach for headgear

runners.name: Kode Grey
runners.spotlight: He has run to a modest level in three maiden/novice runs this year (6f/7f; Tapeta, good to soft) but it would be no surprise were he to take a good step forward now handicapping at a modest level; needs a close look in the betting; yard also runs Luck Money.
runners.comment: Potential improver now handicapping at a modest level; betting to guide

runners.name: Luck Money
runners.spotlight: Shaped okay on her debut in a Leicester maiden that looks a lot stronger now than it did at the time; absent for 181 days but she is open to plenty of improvement now handicapping at a modest level; market useful with the stable running another similar type in Kode Grey.
runners.comment: Promise despite her unappealing form-figures as a 2yo; market useful now handicapping

races.race_name: Racing Welfare Supporting Mental Health Awareness Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Our Guide
runners.spotlight: Joined Jamie Snowden after Irish bumper win in October; made underwhelming stable/hurdle debut in March but justified odds-on favouritism in five-runner Exeter maiden (2m, good) last month and is open to significant further improvement over this longer trip; leading contender.
runners.comment: Won five-runner Exeter maiden over 2m last month; can improve again over this longer trip

runners.name: Ka Mate Du Brivet
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner; tailed off in a bumper on rules debut in February but made a fairly useful rival work quite hard when second of three (albeit well held in the end) over hurdles at Fontwell (2m5f, good) in March; has another big role to play.
runners.comment: Made a fairly useful rival work quite hard when second (well held in end) on hurdle debut

runners.name: Shuil Rocco
runners.spotlight: Bettered hurdle debut when keeping on steadily for modest fourth at Chepstow (2m3f, good) last month; needs to progress again over this longer trip but that's possible.
runners.comment: Kept on steadily for modest fourth at Chepstow last month; further progress possible

runners.name: Savon
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on bumper debut in March and pulled up when 200-1 for last month's hurdle debut at Ffos Las (2m4f, good to soft).
runners.comment: Pulled up when a big-priced outsider for last month's hurdle debut (2m4f)

runners.name: Granite Sands
runners.spotlight: Made a quite encouraging bumper debut in March 2025 but his second bumper run was disappointing and he was also well beaten when 33-1 for last month's hurdle debut at Hereford (2m3f, good).
runners.comment: Well beaten when 33-1 for last month's hurdle debut at Hereford (2m3f)

runners.name: At Sunrise
runners.spotlight: Safely held in two bumpers last spring and pulled up when 300-1 for hurdling debut at Hereford (2m3f, good to soft) in December.
runners.comment: Pulled up when 300-1 for hurdling debut at Hereford (2m3f) in December

runners.name: Tiger Tobin
runners.spotlight: Maiden point winner in 2024; struggled when making rules debut in 3m2f hunter chase last spring and again pulled up when reappearing in a point in February; can only be watched on stable/hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Pulled up in a hunter chase on last year's rules debut and in a point in February

races.race_name: Happy 32nd Birthday Lottie Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Cawthorne Cracker
runners.spotlight: Course winner over hurdles; returned from a break with respectable third in Market Rasen chase (2m1f, good) last month but is 0-5 over fences now and others in this line-up probably have more potential.
runners.comment: Ran okay last month, after a break, but others in this field probably have more potential

runners.name: Whatyouwaitingfor
runners.spotlight: Remote when second of three on stable/chase debut but ran well for a long way before weakening into third at Chepstow (2m, good to soft) last month and could be better suited by the forecast dryer ground here.
runners.comment: Ran well for long way when third at Chepstow last month; dryer ground could suit here

runners.name: Intenzo
runners.spotlight: Below form on last two appearances and his tendency to race too freely remains a worry but he made a promising chase/handicap debut when clear second at Sedgefield (2m, good) in March and that form has been well advertised by the winner since; good claims if back at that level.
runners.comment: Below form on last two starts; good claims if judged on Sedgefield second in March

runners.name: Captain Cool
runners.spotlight: Just 1-9 over hurdles but ended last season with two good seconds at Sedgefield (both 2m1f, good), and last year's Irish point win also bodes well for his prospects on this chasing debut.
runners.comment: Irish point winner; ended last season with two good hurdle runs; chase debut today

runners.name: Definite Dream
runners.spotlight: 0-9 in points (completed only three times) and has same strike-rate over hurdles; gave a fairly good account when fifth of 15 at Worcester (2m, good) last July but this chasing debut comes after a 314-day absence.
runners.comment: Ran quite well on latest hurdling start but makes chase debut after 314-day absence

runners.name: Miss Denver
runners.spotlight: Well-bred mare; made a low-key start to her hurdling career but kept on for respectable third on recent handicap hurdle debut at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good) and could easily have more to offer over fences.
runners.comment: Kept on for third on recent handicap hurdle debut; could have more to offer over fences

races.race_name: Deryck Baker Memorial Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Jena d'Oudairies
runners.spotlight: Led close home to score in first-time blinkers at Taunton (3m, good) last month and is now 2-11 over hurdles; not always one to rely upon heavily but remains well handicapped on older form and unexposed as a stayer.
runners.comment: Led close home in new blinkers last month; still on a good mark and unexposed as a stayer

runners.name: All Inn Hand
runners.spotlight: Won the 2m6f novice hurdle on this card a year ago (good ground) and followed up in handicap chase at Hexham (3m, good to soft) three weeks later, but returned from layoff with lacklustre chase run last month; now reverts to hurdling over new trip and with headgear switched.
runners.comment: Hurdle/chase winner last spring; returned with lacklustre chase run last month

runners.name: Mahon Falls
runners.spotlight: Placed in three Irish maiden hurdles (2m1f-2m5f) for Willie Mullins in the autumn but safely held on both starts for new stable this spring, latterly when upped to 2m7f for handicap debut at Fakenham (good); upped in trip again; needs to raise her game.
runners.comment: Safely held on both stable starts, latterly when upped to 2m7f for handicap debut in April
runners.owner: E W Lewis & Katy Price Racing

runners.name: I Am The Moon
runners.spotlight: Seemingly back in good nick when winning readily at Doncaster (2m3f, soft) in February but ended last season with two lesser efforts (3m/2m7f) and has questions to answer again over this new trip.
runners.comment: Seemingly back in prime form in February but ended last season with two lesser efforts

runners.name: Dunaden Island
runners.spotlight: 1-9 in points; belied triple-digit odds when fourth on rules debut at Wincanton (1m7f, heavy) in February but well beaten twice since (1m7f/2m5f); upped in trip for handicap debut; opening mark demands improvement.
runners.comment: Ex-pointer; fourth at big odds on rules debut but well beaten twice since; handicap debut

races.race_name: William Austin 50 Year Celebration Mares' Handicap Chase (Queen Boudicca Mares' Chase Series Qual')

runners.name: A Little Something
runners.spotlight: Pulled up when making chase debut on heavy ground but runner-up twice on good ground since, latterly at Fontwell (2m3f) last month; gets another chance off the same mark here; obvious contender.
runners.comment: Runner-up off today's mark on last two appearances; likely contender again

runners.name: Little Wren
runners.spotlight: Displayed significant improvement when fitted with cheekpieces for chasing debut at Fakenham (2m5f, good to soft) in January, making all to win by just over 7l; lacked fluency and was pulled up at Plumpton (3m1f, soft) the following month but has strong claims if back on song after 97-day break.
runners.comment: Made all on chasing debut; flopped next time but may still have potential

runners.name: Miss Fedora
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle and dual chase winner when trained by Seamus Mullins; very disappointing joint-favourite when pulled up in a chase for new stable in March and finished down the field over hurdles last month; needs to turn things around.
runners.comment: Started off for new stable with two disappointing runs (chase/hurdle); revival needed

runners.name: Ballyhiho
runners.spotlight: On workable mark if judged on last year's hurdling form and had soft ground as an excuse for below-par run in March two runs back; well beaten over C&D (good) on chasing debut later that month, though, and needs to rebound.
runners.comment: On workable mark if judged on hurdle form; well beaten over C&D on chase debut in March

runners.name: Pure Seduction
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in two Irish points in 2024 and won good-ground maiden hurdle later that year; returned from long absence with two disappointing handicap hurdle runs this year (2m3f/2m; last of six both times), though, and is tricky to weigh up for this chasing debut.
runners.comment: Back from long absence with two underwhelming hurdle runs this year; chase debut today

races.race_name: Boss & The Hoggs Live On The 30th May Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Frank Stamper
runners.spotlight: 0-6 over hurdles but ran well when close second at Fontwell (2m1f, good to soft) last month and is probably still on a workable mark after 1lb nudge; good chance.
runners.comment: 0-6 over hurdles but ran well when close second at Fontwell last month; good chance

runners.name: Reteti
runners.spotlight: 0-13 under all codes but ran well for a long way before weakening into fifth of seven on handicap hurdle debut at Plumpton (2m, soft) in February and ought to be better suited by the forecast faster ground here.
runners.comment: Ran well for a long way on handicap debut and may be better suited by this faster ground

runners.name: Galactic Jack
runners.spotlight: Useful on the Flat in his prime; 0-8 over hurdles but posted best of three runs for new stable (since a long absence) when third at Taunton (2m, good; behind Mi Sueno) last month and is another 1lb lower here; respected.
runners.comment: Best of three runs for new stable when third at Taunton last month; respected

runners.name: Mi Sueno
runners.spotlight: Returned to form with creditable second at Taunton (2m, good) last month, his third start for David Brace, but was pulled up on recent chasing debut and has become hard to predict.
runners.comment: Creditable second over hurdles last month but pulled up in a recent chase; hard to predict

runners.name: Trooper
runners.spotlight: Five-time Flat winner in Belgium/Germany; well beaten in three maiden hurdles this year (last two runs for current stable) but has basement mark for handicap hurdle debut and is well worth a glance in the betting.
runners.comment: Well beaten in three maiden hurdles but won five times on the Flat in Belgium/Germany

runners.name: Blazing Gaden
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on soft-ground handicap debut in March but last month's fifth (behind Mi Sueno and Galactic Jack) at Taunton (2m, good) was more encouraging and further progress is possible, albeit 3lb out of the handicap today.
runners.comment: Well beaten on soft-ground handicap debut but recent good-ground run was more encouraging

runners.name: Prettylady
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in three maiden hurdles for new stable in early part of this year but showed some ability on the Flat when trained in France, and the switch to forecast good ground may enable some improvement on this handicap hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Well beaten in three maiden hurdles this year but may fare better in handicaps

races.race_name: Ladies Day On Sunday 19th July Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: King Roly
runners.spotlight: Won three small-field chases in early part of last season (2m3f-2m4f, good) and ran better than eventual 18l defeat suggests at Perth (2m4f, good; hampered by final-fence faller) last month, after a layoff; will be fine over today's longer trip and goes on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Three chase wins in early part of last season; ran okay last month, after a break; player

runners.name: Tapley
runners.spotlight: 2-10 over fences, the latter win gained over C&D (good) in March; didn't run badly when third of four at Newton Abbot (2m5f, good) last month and seems likely to be on the premises again.
runners.comment: Scored over C&D in March and didn't run badly at Newton Abbot last month; respected

runners.name: Okavango Delta
runners.spotlight: Absent since below-par run in December but returned from break with creditable second at Musselburgh (2m, good to soft; stays much further) in November and won when fresh last spring; has travelled from Northumberland for this race and Sean Bowen is booked; interesting.
runners.comment: Off since December but has good record when fresh and Sean Bowen is booked

runners.name: Grillon De Monty
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner at Worcester (2m7f, good) last summer; absent since heavy defeat over hurdles in November, though, and resumes on fairly tough mark; hooded for first time.
runners.comment: Dual Worcester chase winner last summer; absent since poor hurdle run in November

runners.name: Jubilant
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with emphatic win from the front at Plumpton (3m1f, good to soft), his third chase success, but returned from break with two disappointing runs (chase/hurdle) towards end of last season and has something to prove.
runners.comment: Back from break with two disappointing runs towards end of last season; has bit to prove

runners.name: Arrycan
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish veteran who was safely held on first two starts for new stable this spring (both 3m, good); needs good boost from new cheekpieces here.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish veteran; safely held on both stable starts; cheekpieces now tried

runners.name: Bagheera Ginge
runners.spotlight: Made all in small field at Newton Abbot (3m2f, good) in September but absent since running poorly in Sedgefield marathon three weeks later and needs to resume in peak form to have a chance off current mark; swaps usual cheekpieces for first-time visor.
runners.comment: Off since poor run in October and still on fairly tough mark; headgear switched

races.race_name: Family Day On Sunday 12th July Open Maiden National Hunt Flat Race (Category 3 Elimination) (GBB)

runners.name: Lightsoutandaway
runners.spotlight: Brother to a Listed Flat winner; just a respectable fourth of nine on recent debut at Fontwell (good) but contests what looks another modest race here and ought to have learnt from that experience.
runners.comment: Just a respectable fourth on recent Fontwell debut but ought to have learnt from that

runners.name: Bound For Glory
runners.spotlight: Out of a fair Flat/hurdle winner; fourth on both starts, more recently on good ground at Hereford last month; that form isn't anything to go wild about but he'll probably be on the premises again here.
runners.comment: Fourth on both starts, latterly at Hereford (good) last month; in the mix again here

runners.name: Scotto
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; brother to hurdle/chase winner Brayhill, half-brother to useful hurdler Chooseyourweapon and a point winner; dam pulled up in points, out of a useful bumper/jumps winner; trainer has pretty good bumper strike-rate (17%) and has booked Sean Bowen for this newcomer.
runners.comment: Trainer has good bumper strike-rate (17%) and has booked Sean Bowen for this newcomer

runners.name: Mac's Max
runners.spotlight: Flat-bred 4yo; absent since disappointing run on soft ground in December but made promising debut when second to a subsequent hurdle winner at Newcastle (good to soft) in November, and that performance makes him of strong interest here.
runners.comment: Off since well held in December; big player if judged on promising debut in November

runners.name: Law Of The Jungle
runners.spotlight: Only eighth of nine on debut in France in summer 2024 and pulled up when reappearing for new stable at Taunton (good; 100-1) in March.
runners.comment: Pulled up when 100-1 for British debut at Taunton in March

runners.name: Angel Of My Dreams
runners.spotlight: Made all in a point bumper in March 2025 but well beaten in two rules bumpers last season; not written off just yet but needs addition of hood/tongue-tie to make a big difference.
runners.comment: Won a point bumper on debut but well beaten in both subsequent rules bumpers

races.race_name: Preakness Stakes (Grade 1) (Dirt)

runners.name: Ocelli
runners.spotlight: Late-running maiden, much improved with blinkers removed last two starts; ran a huge race when 70-1 for Kentucky Derby last time, making a big move around home turn and looking the likely winner in straight until edging left and tiring, still finishing just 1l third to Golden Tempo; tough to beat if reproducing that on fourth start in nine weeks (also needs clear run from inside and another pace collapse) and must enter considerations.
runners.comment: Maiden; came from behind when 70-1 third in Kentucky Derby; good chance with clear run
runners.owner: Ashley Durr, Anthony Tate & Front Page Equestrian LLC

runners.name: Taj Mahal
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten in three, all here in 2026, including black-type Miracle Wood (1m, muddy; by neck) In February and Federico Tesio (1m1f) in April; in the latter he set torrid pace, opened up again when pressed turning in and stayed on strongly to win by 8l; no winner of that event has followed up in this since 1983, but this colt has powerful owners, good looks, a good pedigree and lots of stamina; should get run of race sitting behind Napoleon Solo and surely must go well.
runners.comment: Unbeaten in three here, including black-type events by neck and 8l; strong claims
runners.owner: SF Racing LLC, Starlight Racing Et Al

runners.name: Incredibolt
runners.spotlight: Progressive juvenile in 2025, winning Grade 3 Street Sense at Churchill Downs; impressive winner of black-type Virginia Derby (1m1f; stayed on strongly and surged clear last furlong, Ocelli beaten 7l in sixth) at Colonial Downs in March, then good 4l sixth to Golden Tempo in Kentucky Derby, staying on dourly having raced closer to strong pace than first three finishers; thriving and one of the few with no serious stamina questions.
runners.comment: Progressive colt, staying-on 4l sixth in Kentucky Derby; should give another solid account

runners.name: Chip Honcho
runners.spotlight: Best when able to dominate (pulls hard when taken on) as when second, pair clear of Golden Tempo, to unbeaten Paladin in Grade 2 Risen Star at Fair Grounds in February; rested since fifth in Grade 2 Louisiana Derby (9.5f) there last time and reportedly training well; Jose Ortiz resumes the mount (rode him to maiden score) but claims rest on whether he settles better.
runners.comment: Grade 2 second to Paladin in February is good form; pulled too hard latest; claims
runners.owner: Leland Ackerley Racing LLC, James Sherwood et al

runners.name: Iron Honor
runners.spotlight: Game winner (first two clear) of Grade 3 Gotham at Aqueduct in February; drawn widest and bumped first turn when well beaten in Grade 2 Wood Memorial (1m1f) on same track last time, racing too freely and weakening; trainer Chad Brown (two Preakness winners) takes blinkers off and engages Flavien Prat; progressing well before the Wood and likely to do a lot better this time; dangerous to all.
runners.comment: Good Grade 3 winner in February; drawn wide and bumped in Wood Memorial; dangerous
runners.owner: St Elias Stable, William H Lawrence & Glassman Racing

runners.name: Napoleon Solo
runners.spotlight: Impressive winner of Grade 1 Champagne at Aqueduct as juvenile; foot issues since and only fifth in Grade 2 events at Gulfstream and Aqueduct (Wood Memorial) in 2026, albeit setting too strong a pace last time and still beaten under 3l; reportedly training brilliantly and will make bold bid from front.
runners.comment: Grade 1 winner as juvenile but only fifth in two Grade 2s this year; could bounce back

runners.name: Robusta
runners.spotlight: Easily best effort in six-race career when head second to Potente in Grade 2 San Felipe at Santa Anita in March; disappointing since, including from widest draw in Kentucky Derby when never able to challenge, plodding on and always towards rear; hard to support with any confidence.
runners.comment: Touched off in Grade 2 in March but well beaten twice since, including in Kentucky Derby

runners.name: The Hell We Did
runners.spotlight: Promising efforts in sprints on his opening three starts, notably when impressive allowance winner at Sunland Park in March; first race at 8.5f (bred to stay) when fair 2l second in Grade 3 Lexington at Keeneland last time, staying on steadily but swept aside by longshot winner; needs more.
runners.comment: Fair 2l second in Grade 3 at Keeneland (first go at 8.5f) but this is a much tougher test

runners.name: Crupper
runners.spotlight: Son of top-class stallion Candy Ride and improving, but needs to step on hugely on his 1m1f black-type win at Oaklawn Park in April, when he got home gamely in blanket finish; victory today would be a sizeable shock, even in an open year, but he could improve for the increased test of stamina.
runners.comment: Got home in blanket finish to black-type event (1m1f) at Oaklawn Park; outsider

runners.name: Talkin
runners.spotlight: Good winner of Saratoga maiden before second (6l behind Napoleon Solo) in Grade 1 Champagne at Aqueduct as 2yo; has not progressed, though respectable 13l third in Grade 1 Blue Grass Stakes at Keeneland latest start; hard to make top choice, even with Irad Ortiz in the plate.
runners.comment: Gets Irad Ortiz but needs improvement on 13l third in Grade 1 at Keeneland latest
runners.owner: Reeves Thoroughbred Racing, Pine Racing Stables Et Al

runners.name: Bull By The Horns
runners.spotlight: Well held in Grade 2 Fountain Of Youth Stakes at Gulfstream before winning 8.5f black-type event on Tapeta at Turfway Park by half a length (pace collapse, came from 20l off pace); that form leaves him with lots to find, but he is at least progressing nicely; will get far behind.
runners.comment: Narrow winner of black-type event on Tapeta surface at Turfway; needs to find a lot more

runners.name: Great White
runners.spotlight: Narrow winner of Listed race on Tapeta at Turfway Park, then well-beaten fifth in Grade 1 Blue Grass at Keeneland; has to prove he acts on dirt and he was withdrawn before start of Kentucky Derby after rearing and falling in starting-gate area.
runners.comment: Listed winner on Tapeta surface but no comparable form on dirt; has to prove he belongs

runners.name: Pretty Boy Miah
runners.spotlight: Speedy type who has impressed over shorter in maiden (6.5f) and allowance race (1m) at Aqueduct; talented, but unlikely to prove suited by step up in trip and he's drawn widest.
runners.comment: Stiff task after maiden (6.5f) and allowance (1m) wins at Aqueduct over shorter; wide draw
runners.owner: Team Penney Racing, Echo Racing Et Al

runners.name: Corona De Oro
runners.spotlight: Likeable maiden winner at Fair Grounds (1m) before third in Grade 3 Lexington at Keeneland last time, less than 1l behind The Hell We Did but finishing weakly; did not seem to relish that step up to 8.5f and has questions to answer over another furlong here.
runners.comment: Third in Grade 3 Lexington (8.5f) last time when finished weakly; stamina/class questions
runners.owner: On Our Own Stable LLC, Commonwealth Stable Et Al

races.race_name: Victoria Mile (Grade 1) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)
Buffalo Hill Handicap (C+3 Course) (Turf)

runners.name: Rosewood Fleetfoot
runners.spotlight: Behind some of these on dirt last time; might be happier on turf but only placed once on this surface in the past; did win a recent trial.
runners.comment: Latest defeat was on dirt but turf exploits aren't overly compelling
runners.owner: Lam Ka Lai & Yeung Wing Shut

runners.name: Perfect Pairing
runners.spotlight: 1-31 in Hong Kong and peak form has been over further; still went off favourite over this far at Happy Valley last time and didn't get the best of runs; needs respecting.
runners.comment: Favourite over this far at Happy Valley last time and didn't get the clearest of runs
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Jerry Chow Shiu Ming

runners.name: Perfecto Moments
runners.spotlight: Reserve; no wins in 18 but has threatened and was only 2l away at Happy Valley last time (behind Family Fortune) despite being forced wide on the final bend; each-way claims if getting in.
runners.comment: Only 2l away at Happy Valley last time despite being forced wide on the final bend

runners.name: Gold Tack
runners.spotlight: Poor strike-rate and beaten 4l here in April; underwhelming in trials on either side of that.
runners.comment: Beaten 4l here in April; underwhelming in trials on either side of that
runners.owner: Robert Chung Wai Shu & Jeanette Chung Siu Wah

runners.name: Chateau Le Peche
runners.spotlight: Only win in 26 starts came over 1m2f here more than a year ago; needs to fare better than of late.
runners.comment: Only win came over 1m2f here more than a year ago; needs to fare better than of late

runners.name: Run Yes Run
runners.spotlight: Has essentially become disappointing and he's unproven over this far.
runners.comment: Has essentially become disappointing and he's unproven over this far
runners.owner: Kong Wing Ming & Edmond Kong Chun Man

runners.name: Matsu Victor
runners.spotlight: He's been regressing for a while but handicapped accordingly and his last run here wasn't too shoddy; well drawn and having his first crack at a Class 5, he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Regressive but mark has bottomed out and he's having his first crack at a Class 5
runners.owner: Lau Ying Kin & Lau Mok Yuen Kee

runners.name: Special Hedge
runners.spotlight: Another who has been running in Class 4s but without much promise of late; C&D winner last October off 5lb higher but reservations for now.
runners.comment: Another who has been running in Class 4s but without much promise of late

runners.name: All Are Mine
runners.spotlight: Hard to win with and can be up and down, but he was a rallying third under Zac Purton over 1m1f on dirt here four weeks ago and that was from a much wider stall (Hailtothevictors was fourth); has comparable form on turf so has to be considered.
runners.comment: Third under Purton over this far on dirt last time and that was from a much wider stall

runners.name: Happy Buddies
runners.spotlight: Reserve; no places in nine starts and still looks on a high enough mark.
runners.comment: Reserve; no places in nine starts and still looks on a high enough mark

runners.name: Kolachi
runners.spotlight: Yet to win in Hong Kong but wasn't far away over Happy Valley's 1m three starts back and this is a welcome drop in grade; stall 13 is far from ideal, however.
runners.comment: Runs well now and again, and he's dropped in grade; stall 13 is far from ideal, however

runners.name: Family Fortune
runners.spotlight: 6lb higher than for his narrow win at Happy Valley (1m) but seemed to dispel any stamina doubts over this far when a rallying third last time; the principal concern is stall 14.
runners.comment: Has found some form at Happy Valley but returns here with a wicked draw to deal with

runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; drawn in 3 and Nichola Yuen takes off 10lb, so hard to dismiss

runners.name: Hailtothevictors
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough over 1m here in April and has continued his good work on dirt with a couple of fourths; effective on turf and high on the shortlist despite a wide stall.

runners.name: Super Hong Kong

runners.comment: Multiple turf winner but he has looked flat in two runs after a break

races.race_name: Cheung Shan Handicap (C+3 Course) (Turf)

runners.name: Happy United

runners.comment: Reserve; the odd run offers hope and he's another who has been short on luck at times

runners.name: Zouper Fellow
runners.spotlight: Responded well to the fitting of cheekpieces when making virtually all over C&D last month but the 7lb rise appeared to find him out next time when behind Run Run Sunrise.
runners.comment: 7lb rise appeared to find him out last time when behind Run Run Sunrise
runners.owner: Dr Philip Ho Kin Hoi, Jason Ho Chung Man Et Al

runners.name: Lahore
runners.spotlight: Only fifth in his recent trials but had responded well to the visor when close up in two C&D races earlier this year and Zac Purton takes over this time.
runners.comment: Recent trial runs underwhelming but twice ran really well here earlier in the year

runners.name: Comet Radiance

runners.comment: Close second to Run Run Sunrise here in March but hasn't kicked on at all

runners.comment: Effective over 5f and 6f, here and at Happy Valley; fair mark and in fair form

runners.comment: 3-3 in trials before a close third here last week when always up with the pace

runners.name: Run Run Sunrise
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and likeably consistent over C&D, including when third in the added hood four weeks ago; same mark here but with Nichola Yuen taking away 10lb; respected.
runners.comment: Lightly raced and likeably consistent over C&D; Nichola Yuen now takes away 10lb
runners.owner: Tang Ming, James Tang Sau Yip Et Al

runners.name: Cloud Nine
runners.spotlight: All runs over 6f and bang there in three of his races, twice here; can be forgiven latest blip on dirt debut from a poor draw; in the mix.
runners.comment: Form claims for sure and forgiven latest blip on dirt debut from a poor draw

runners.name: Mabubu
runners.spotlight: Won on debut here over 6f but since well held over that trip and 7f; has won a recent trial and he does seem quick enough for 5f.
runners.comment: Recent trial winner so could bounce back and seems quick enough for this sharper test

runners.name: Precision Mind
runners.spotlight: Has won trials but hasn't offered a lot in his two official races, here over 5f and 6f.
runners.comment: Has won trials but hasn't offered a lot in his two official races, here over 5f and 6f
runners.owner: Dr & Mrs Allan Chau Kit Ho And Mr & Mrs Chau Man Kwok

runners.name: Flowing
runners.spotlight: Hasn't been showing enough in his trials to expect too much on official debut.
runners.comment: Hasn't been showing enough in his trials to expect too much on official debut

runners.spotlight: Reserve; winner of two trials and when seventh here last week (behind Bustling City) he got no luck in running when odds of 17-5; if getting a run he'd be of definite interest.
runners.comment: Reserve; winner of two trials and got no luck when seventh here last week

runners.name: Country Pride

runners.comment: Debutant with form figures of 52633 in trials; well drawn and he has some level of ability

runners.spotlight: Progressed to win his last trial but probably best watched off this opening mark.
runners.comment: Progressed to win his last trial but probably best watched off this opening mark
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Wong Kwun Keong, Wong Men Chu & Wong Bo Chu

runners.name: Kingdom
runners.spotlight: Has created a good impression in winning two recent trials, the latest under Joao Moreira; stall 1 could be troublesome but he has potential.
runners.comment: Has created a good impression in winning two recent trials, the latest under Moreira

runners.name: Ruby Thrive
runners.spotlight: Finished last on his debut over 6f here in November; mixed bag in trials subsequently, winning once.
runners.comment: Finished last on his debut over 6f here in November; mixed bag in trials subsequently
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Michael Chan Se Ping, Adam C Y Man & A C Y Lung

races.race_name: Eagle's Nest Handicap (C+3 Course) (Turf)

runners.name: Blue Baron

runners.comment: Beaten just over 1l with no excuses over this far at Happy Valley last time
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Mark Fong Chung, Matthew Fong & Victoria Fong

runners.comment: Yet to win but beaten only a length in two of last four races, here and at Happy Valley

runners.comment: Didn't run badly last time given 5f is too sharp and he won a recent trial

runners.name: Majestic Delight
runners.spotlight: Regular here over 6f; not at all disgraced in his last two races (behind Triumphant Warrior latest) but that 6lb rise for winning in March might be anchoring him.
runners.comment: In form but that 6lb rise for winning in March might be anchoring him

runners.name: Turf Phoenix

runners.comment: Stable debut when placed at Happy Valley and may have turned a corner

runners.name: Happy Boys
runners.spotlight: 0-16; didn't get the best of runs on stable debut at Happy Valley but couldn't call him unlucky.
runners.comment: Didn't get the best of runs on stable debut at Happy Valley but couldn't call him unlucky
runners.owner: David Tse Sik Hung, Allison Tse Pui Shan & Q Tse Pui Ching

runners.name: Ruby Sailing

runners.comment: Won twice over C&D; won a recent trial but he's inconsistent and wouldn't be progressive
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Michael Chan Se Ping

runners.comment: Reserve; back over 6f and his encouraging penultimate effort was over 7f

runners.comment: Eye-catching two runs back and a wide stall scuppered him last time

runners.name: No Other Choice

runners.comment: Interesting on more than one of his runs and Moreira has a good draw to work with
runners.owner: Cheng Chung Wah & Enzo Cheng Tin Hang

runners.name: Happy Action
runners.spotlight: Reserve; still to win but made late gains for third from a good draw here 11 days ago; possible if getting in.
runners.comment: Reserve; still to win but made late gains for third from a good draw here 11 days ago

runners.comment: C&D winner and then came from well off the pace to chase home an improver

runners.name: Little Monster

runners.comment: Some 4l behind Papaya Brose three runs back and no significant improvement since

runners.comment: Reserve; has dropped to a realistic mark but hasn't been running that well for a while
runners.owner: Cheng Wan Wai, Sheena Cheng Tsz Yan & Selina Cheng Tsz Ching

runners.comment: Last couple of runs not his best, particularly the latest when well drawn

runners.name: Oldtown

runners.comment: Can be up and down; fairly quiet on recent yard debut

runners.comment: Steady progress in his trials and only faded late on when third over C&D three weeks ago

runners.name: Viva Boss
runners.spotlight: Had a tough draw last time but doesn't look one to be making excuses for.
runners.comment: Had a tough draw last time but doesn't look one to be making excuses for

runners.name: Regrowth Winner

runners.comment: Down the field on his debut in April and since beaten in a trial; others preferred

runners.comment: Drawn wide for debut and has finished fifth and second in his trials; best watched

runners.spotlight: Modest in his trials and looks no better on the track; readily opposed.
runners.comment: Modest in his trials and looks no better on the track; readily opposed

runners.comment: Insufficient promise thus far to believe he'll be making a splash on this occasion

runners.comment: Has performed okay in his trials without managing to win one; Moreira booked

runners.name: Bienvenue

runners.comment: Had some of these behind when runner-up here last time; has a similar draw

runners.owner: Jimmy Tang Kwok Leung, Ho Kai Man & Ricky Lee Wing Ngai

runners.comment: It's been a low-key start in Hong Kong, though was second in a trial last week

runners.name: Master Champion
runners.spotlight: His competitive runs last autumn were in Class 4s and he's struggled upped in grade; fit from trials, finishing runner-up in two of the five.
runners.comment: Competitive in trials since last official run but seems best at Class 4 level

runners.name: High Praise

runners.comment: One run in Australia in 2024; 323 in his recent trials and Moreira takes the ride

runners.spotlight: Progressive in Australia late last year with two wins over further; could have fared better in his local trials and likely best watched.
runners.comment: Won twice in Australia; could have fared better in his trials and likely best watched

runners.name: Absolute Honour
runners.spotlight: Campaign mainly over 6f/7f since the autumn but won quite comfortably under Zac Purton when returned to 1m here four weeks ago; big player if today's even longer trip suits.
runners.comment: Won quite comfortably over 1m here last month; big player if this longer trip suits

runners.name: Kingly Demeanor

runners.name: Romantic Fantasy

runners.comment: Has modest strike-rate but scores high marks for his consistency this year

runners.name: Star Brose
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with 1m3f win and was placed on consecutive starts in March (1m3f/1m2f); not quite at best last month and deserted by Zac Purton today but still enters calculations.
runners.comment: Placed twice in March; not quite at same level last time but still enters calculations

runners.comment: Has struggled on all three outings since 75-1 fourth here (1m) on New Year's Day

runners.comment: 0-7 in Hong Kong but has been competitive in Class 3 this year; considered in this grade

runners.name: Star Elegance

runners.comment: 0-7 but went close over 1m in March; could have a say under good 10lb claimer Nichola Yuen

runners.comment: Well down the field on last two outings; needs to have been refreshed by two-month break

runners.name: State Security

runners.comment: Unable to pose a significant threat on first two starts (7f/1m)
runners.owner: Marc Chan & Lily Chan Li Lei

runners.name: Grand Turbo

runners.comment: Displayed huge improvement when big-odds fourth over C&D last month, his fourth start

runners.name: Serangoon

runners.comment: Ex-Australian 4yo who has struggled to get competitive on all four HK starts this year

runners.comment: Yet fully to convince stamina-wise over 7f but on good mark and enters calculations

runners.comment: 11-race maiden; didn't fire when well drawn in January; returns from a break

runners.spotlight: 0-16; gave good account when third over 1m here last month and should be okay back at 7f but wide draw dents his appeal.
runners.comment: Ran well when placed over 1m last month but wide drawn dents appeal

runners.name: Silver Up
runners.spotlight: Placed over C&D on seasonal debut in October but signed off for Michael Chang with two lesser efforts last autumn; makes stable debut after 168-day break; market helpful.
runners.comment: Placed over C&D in October; makes stable debut after 168-day break; market helpful
runners.owner: Eden Wong Chi Hong & Anita Chu Suk Ching

runners.name: Joltin
runners.spotlight: Looked promising when winning 2yo maiden in Ireland last year but has struggled to make an impact in Hong Kong this year (five runs).
runners.comment: Won as a 2yo in Ireland but has struggled to make an impact in Hong Kong this year
runners.owner: Jack Chan Hoi, Brian Pong Chung Bon & Xin Yi

runners.name: Ace
runners.spotlight: Made winning stable debut in Class 5 C&D contest in March and was creditable fourth from stall 13 in this higher grade last month; could still have more to offer for new trainer; high on the list.
runners.comment: C&D winner on stable debut and ran well from tough draw last time; high on the list

runners.name: Proud Box
runners.spotlight: Delivered on earlier promise when making all for clearcut C&D win last month, his eighth start; hiked up 10lb for that but is well drawn to attack and further progress is possible.
runners.comment: Hiked up 10lb for last month's clearcut C&D win but well drawn to attack from the front
runners.owner: Leung Lun Ping & Asther Leung Wing Yi

runners.name: Gratifide
runners.spotlight: Won when based in Australia but has made a disappointing start to his Hong Kong career; eased in grade today.
runners.comment: Won when based in Australia but has made a disappointing start to his Hong Kong career
runners.owner: Wong Leung Sau Hing & Eddie Wong Ming Chak

runners.name: Bucephalas
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D in November but has badly lost his way in recent months, and was particularly disappointing on latest outing.
runners.comment: Has badly lost his way in recent months, and was particularly disappointing last time

runners.name: High Rise Victory
runners.spotlight: Towards rear throughout when a 95-1 outsider for debut here (6f) three weeks ago.
runners.comment: Towards rear throughout when a 95-1 outsider for debut here (6f) three weeks ago

runners.name: Prestige Superior
runners.spotlight: 2-6 when based in Australia but a down-the-field outsider on all seven Hong Kong starts; very difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: A down-the-field outsider on all seven Hong Kong starts; very difficult to enthuse over

runners.name: Strathpeffer
runners.spotlight: Close second over C&D on consecutive starts after winning here in October but form dipped in February/March, and his run of poor luck on the drawn front has continued.
runners.comment: Second twice since C&D win in October but form dipped on last two appearances

runners.name: Superb Kid
runners.spotlight: Reserve; three-time C&D winner last season; hinted at possible return to form when fifth over 1m in February but has largely been disappointing for new stable this year.
runners.comment: Largely disappointing for new stable this year

runners.name: Meaningful Dragon
runners.spotlight: Came good with ready C&D win last July, his sixth start, but wasn't seen again until a poor performance in January and has been off for another 112 days since; drawn on the outside; others look safer.
runners.comment: C&D winner last July but not seen again until running poorly in January; others look safer
runners.owner: Peter Chu Ka Lok, Maria Teresa Chu & Dr C Chu Ping Wing

races.race_name: Prix Alain du Breil - Gde Course de Haies de Printemps des Quatre Ans (Hurdle) (Grade 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Selma De Vary
runners.spotlight: Smart filly who returns to France having run three good races in Grade 1s for Willie Mullins, albeit racing keenly not helping her cause; was 7l behind Delmegan off levels here last March (1m7f, heavy) and then behind that rival again in a Grade 3, but she's improved since and the new trip is an interesting move.
runners.comment: Smart filly who returns to France having run 3 good races in Grade 1s for Willie Mullins

runners.name: Delmegan
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to some really good jumpers, notably Goshen and Elimay; hard to knock a filly in such good form, and she holds a few of these on various pieces of form, not least her success in last month's Grade 2 Prix Amadou over C&D;  should be a big player.
runners.comment: Well bred and won the Grade 2 Prix Amadou over C&D recently from plenty of these

runners.name: Leopard Du Berlais
runners.spotlight: Proved to be the one to beat in 2025, numbering the Grade 1 Prix Cambaceres (2m2f, heavy) here among his wins but third as a 1-10 shot on his return this year behind Edamura before falling about mid-race in the Prix Amadou, which featured a number of these, last time, having gone off odds-on; would set good standard if finding his best again.
runners.comment: Has some questions to answer but is a Grade 1 winner
runners.owner: Ecurie Hugo & Pierre Pilarski & Haras Du Berlais

runners.name: Edamura
runners.spotlight: Won a Listed hurdle and two Grade 3 events, one here over 2m2f in March when the 1-10 shot Leopard Du Berlais was in third, and wasn't beaten that far in the Prix Amadou last month; needs more but could well get into the places.
runners.comment: Has Graded wins in his profile but extra needed on Prix Amadou run

runners.name: Isaac Of York
runners.spotlight: Won a couple of times at Cagnes-Sur-Mer late 2025/early 2026 but was more than 5l behind Delmegan during March in a Grade 3 here (while getting 3lb) over 2m2f; stepped up on that over C&D recently, albeit still getting 3lb from Delmegan, so shouldn't beat that rival here on these terms unless she doesn't have a clear round.
runners.comment: Produced a career-best last time but needs more to beat Delmegan

runners.name: Mountain Ridge
runners.spotlight: Course winner last year, who recorded a career-best when third in the Grade 2 Prix Amadou over C&D 28 days ago, when behind a couple of these, having previously only been a length behind Delmegan again prior to that in a Grade 3, while getting weight from that rival; unexposed at the trip so might be capable of better.
runners.comment: Found Delmegan too good two times in a row; might be capable of better

runners.name: Killing Bid
runners.spotlight: Nicely bred gelding, who beat Edamura by over 6l in a conditions hurdle here in September (2m1f 110yds, heavy) but hasn't really gone on from that and was beaten more than 9l recently when fifth behind a number of these in the Prix Amadou over C&D having led for a long time; others make stronger appeal.
runners.comment: Behind a few of these last time without excuses; others easier to fancy

runners.name: Louminos
runners.spotlight: Having his 14th start but heads into this off a career-best in landing a Listed handicap over C&D recently but, that said, hard to make a case for him in this company considering he was a 120-1 and beaten 22l in last November's Grade 1 Prix Cambaceres.
runners.comment: Produced a career-best in a Listed handicap last time, but this is far harder

runners.name: Maharaja
runners.spotlight: Yet to win a race and was almost 3l behind Louminos 22 days ago (giving him 6lb) in a C&D Listed handicap, which all suggests he'll require a big career-best to make this his first success.
runners.comment: Hasn't won a race yet, so big career-best needed to make this his first success

races.race_name: Le Defi des Haras - Grand Steeple-Chase de Paris (Grade 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Gold Tweet
runners.spotlight: Finished sixth in this in 2024 and a bit inconsistent after until posting a big figure in the Gold Cup and then landing the Grade 2 Prix Ingre here in added cheekpieces (2m6f, very soft) from Toscana Du Berlais, while getting 8lb from that rival (more of these behind); stays well, so holds claims if headgear works again.
runners.comment: Won the Grade 2 Prix Ingre here last time, beating plenty of these in added cheekpieces
runners.owner: Agv Karwin Stud, Gabriel Leenders Et Al

runners.name: Kaadam
runners.spotlight: Went on a good run of form as a chaser in 2024, the highlight being success in the Grade 1 Prix Maurice Gillois (2m6f, very soft) but not been quite as good since, with finishing a race the main problem recently; stamina has yet to be fully tested but was moving well when falling two out in last year's Grade 1 Prix la Haye Jousselin (3m3f110yds, heavy); loads of talent but jumping the concern.
runners.comment: Grade 1 winner who has the talent but jumping a concern recently
runners.owner: Simon Macauley, Ecurie Hub De Montmirail & Andrew Peake

runners.name: Toscana Du Berlais
runners.spotlight: Improved with age, running well plenty of times since finishing fourth in this 12 months ago (fell in 2024), most notably recording a career-best when landing the Grade 1 Prix la Haye Jousselin (3m3f110yds, heavy) in November; last run behind Gold Tweet was encouraging considering he gave that rival weight and should be involved.
runners.comment: Stays well and building nicely towards this; should be involved
runners.owner: Ecurie Hub De Montmirail, Haras Du Berlais & Scea Equi Flore

runners.name: Kolokico
runners.spotlight: Classy performer who won the Grade 1 Prix Ferdinand Dufaure (2m6f, very soft) on this card in 2024; run well plenty of times since, including when second in this 12 months ago, and shaped nicely in the Prix Murat, albeit looking tired late on, when last seen, a race that contained a few of these; sound claims.
runners.comment: Second last year and ran well in his prep; sound claims
runners.owner: Lord Daresbury, Ecurie Mme Jacques Cypres & Nicolas Cypres

runners.name: In Love
runners.spotlight: Produced a career-best when fourth in this in 2024 but well beaten seventh 12 months ago and generally struggled in various company since; could never land a blow in a premier handicap at Aintree last time as a 50-1 chance, so major upturn in form required with tongue-tie returning.
runners.comment: Produced a career-best when fourth in this in 2024 but well beaten seventh 12 months ago
runners.owner: Andrew Peake, Simon Macauley & Bryant Family

runners.name: Juntos Ganamos
runners.spotlight: Unseated when favourite for this race in 2024 and only won victory race since; behind a few of these in November's Grade 1 Prix la Haye Jousselin (3m3f110yds, heavy), mistakes not helping, and not done that well in two starts this year, although they came over shorter; plenty of ability but comes with risks; cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: Capable on his day but needs to improve for the addition of cheekpieces on recent form

runners.name: Sel Jem
runners.spotlight: Won this race back in 2022 but had a couple of big absences from the track since; beaten 31l in this last year but still more than capable of smart form, although probably vulnerable to younger rivals; trip clearly no problem, so has top-four potential.
runners.comment: Vulnerable to younger rivals but might be staying on when others fade

runners.name: Bon Garcon
runners.spotlight: Won a Grade 2 chase (2m6f, very soft) here almost a year ago but doesn't head into this in great heart, following a fall in the Grade 2 Prix Murat with a well-beaten eighth in the Prix Ingre 22 days ago, a race that featured a lot of these; marathon trip needs to bring about a lot of improvement.
runners.comment: Won at Grade 2 level, but not been at his best and this will be a new trip

runners.name: Kentucky Wood
runners.spotlight: Finished fourth in last year's Grade 1 Grande Course de Haies d'Auteuil (3m1f110yds, very soft) here but hasn't done enough as a chaser to suggest he's up to landing a race like this considering his only win over fences was over two years ago and he was behind plenty of these in the Prix Ingre 22 days ago.
runners.comment: Capable on his day but vulnerable up against classy opposition
runners.owner: Alain Jathiere, Franck Deliberos & Haras De Saint-Voir

runners.name: Karre D'As
runners.spotlight: Only run ten times but has won three Grade 3 chases here at up to 2m6f; struggled at a higher level so far, which suggests this new marathon trip will need to bring about quite a bit of improvement.
runners.comment: Won three Grade 3 chases but struggled in a higher grade so far; new trip

runners.name: Crown Du Berlais
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to Sire Du Berlais among others; won a 2m6f Grade 3 chase here in December and has run better on the figures since, but was over well behind Gold Tweet, and others, in the recent Grade 2 Prix Ingre; another in the field who needs plenty more for a new trip.
runners.comment: Comfortably held by a few of these last time; new trip needs to improve him
runners.owner: Ec Hugo & Pierre Pilarski, Europ Horses & Haras Du Berlais

runners.name: Kapaca De Thaix
runners.spotlight: Knows how to get his head in front (six wins from 17 starts) and did stay 3m2f110yds well enough at Pau in January, but hasn't won a Graded event yet, so needs this extra distance to improve him quite a bit to add to his tally.
runners.comment: Knows how to win but needs new trip to improve him on the figures

runners.name: Kivala Du Berlais
runners.spotlight: Notably a half-brother to Kalif Du Berlais; interesting runner on his best form, most notably his win in the Grade 1 Prix Alain du Breil (hurdle) success last year plus a success over a few of these in the Prix Murat (2m6f, heavy) in March on his chasing debut, staying on powerfully in the home straight; very interesting if stamina holds out over this trip.
runners.comment: Beat a few of these on his chasing debut 50 days ago; very interesting

runners.name: Le Petit Cheval
runners.spotlight: Winner of seven of his 18 starts and was last seen beating Kaadam 6l when landing a Grade 3 here (2m7f110yds, soft) while getting 11lb; this contest is something else, however, and he needs to prove he can stay this trip.
runners.comment: Heads into this in good form but never attempted a marathon trip before

races.race_name: Prix Ferdinand Dufaure (Chase) (Grade 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Shannon Maestro
runners.spotlight: Placed in the Grade 1 Prix Cambaceres (hurdle) last November and improved with experience as a chaser, winning the Grade 3 (2m6f, heavy) that Metronomique fell in before finishing 2l second to Duke Senam in a Grade 2 while giving that rival 5lb; solid chance.
runners.comment: Has met a few of these already and done enough to suggest he has a solid chance

runners.name: Metronomique
runners.spotlight: Won five of his eight starts, four of which have been over fences, albeit the loss came on his only visit to this track when he fell early; readily won at Compiegne since (where all his success has come) and obvious claims if completing the course and 2m6f isn't an issue.
runners.comment: Fell early on his only visit here but looked smart other than that as a chaser
runners.owner: John Cavanagh, Mme Sharon Colleen Nelson et al

runners.name: Duke Senam
runners.spotlight: Related to plenty of good horses; mostly ordinary form earlier in his career but made significant improvement for a step up to 2m6f for the Grade 2 Prix Jean Stern on very soft last time, beating Shannon Maestro (while getting 5lb) for a first success, with a couple more of these behind; needs more to confirm that result off levels but he'd unexposed at trip.
runners.comment: Beat a few of these in the Grade 2 Prix Jean Stern last time; unexposed at the trip
runners.owner: Detre Racing, Jacques Detre & Ecurie Team Spirit

runners.name: Good Girl De Faust
runners.spotlight: Taken fairly well to chasing, beating Duke Senam here over 2m1f110yds in March (Mamaland third) before finding Duke Senam too good over this C&D in the Grade 2 Prix Jean Stern 28 days ago having been in front for a while; hood comes off and may do better.
runners.comment: Produced her best effort in the C&D Prix Jean Stern last time; may do better
runners.owner: Martin Ingram, M Seror Et Al

runners.name: Duc De Roche
runners.spotlight: Capable of smart form but was behind Shannon Maestro two runs ago in a C&D Grade 3 and probably wasn't going to win the Grade 2 Prix Jean Stern last time (which featured plenty of these) when getting the last wrong; hard to dismiss completely but more required.
runners.comment: Capable of smart form and hard to dismiss completely but more required
runners.owner: Alain Jathiere, Christian Geoffroy & Francois Nicolle

runners.name: Victrix Du Mesnil
runners.spotlight: Only run four times, failing to win as a hurdler but scoring first-time up as a chaser in a handicap here over 2m1f110yds; this looks a tough task but open to lots of improvement and unexposed at the trip; no hood this time
runners.comment: Won only start as a chaser, over shorter, and this is much harder; new trip

runners.name: Melisse Du Mathan
runners.spotlight: Placed in a 2m2f Grade 2 as a hurdler and taken well to chasing, 2-2 since going over fences, landing a Listed race here 38 days ago over 2m1f110yds on very soft; needs more on the figures but new trip could help with that.
runners.comment: Two from two as a chaser but needs more on the figures; new trip may help with that
runners.owner: Ecurie Du Mathan, A Chaille-Chaille & F Pamart

runners.name: Mamaland
runners.spotlight: Showed useful form in three hurdling starts and shaping nicely over fences, winning her most recent start by 6l but more than 2l behind Good Girl De Faust prior to that, when Duke Senam was runner-up; more improvement likely, especially if she stays this new trip.
runners.comment: Progressing as a chaser and could improve again if staying the trip

runners.name: Quonstantine
runners.spotlight: Won a couple of races at Cagnes-Sur-Mer at the start of her career, including a chase, but held since, including when almost 8l behind Shannon Maestro in a C&D Grade 3 here in March, albeit slightly hampered two out by a faller; others easier to fancy.
runners.comment: Held since two wins at start of her career; tough task with  Shannon Maestro

races.race_name: Enter ITV7 For Free EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Mind Those Steps
runners.spotlight: 7-1 and green, promising third of seven in novice at Ripon (6f, good) on his debut 22 days ago, running on late; this Pinatubo colt rates a player with better to come.
runners.comment: A promising debut third at Ripon, running on late; this Pinatubo colt rates a player

runners.name: Location Location
runners.spotlight: 14-1, encouraging debut fifth of ten in 6f Kempton novice 17 days ago, finishing well having been short of room over 1f out; this Harry Angel gelding seems sure to do better.
runners.comment: Encouraging debut fifth at Kempton, finishing well; seems sure to do better

runners.name: Oh So Slow Sloth
runners.spotlight: 75,000gns yearling; half-sister to 6f/7.5f winner Brighton Boy (RPR 90); dam, runner-up over 6f (RPR 70), is a half-sister to winners Zanetto (6f Listed) and Dream Tune (useful 7f), out of maiden half-sister to 1m1f Group 2 winner Alkhaadem; this Dark Angel filly needs considering, especially if the market vibes are positive.
runners.comment: This Dark Angel filly needs considering, especially if the market vibes are positive

runners.name: This Moment
runners.spotlight: Showcasing half-brother to winners Dickensian (Group-placed 5f 2yo; RPR 97) and Time Turner (12.4f; RPR 69); dam, 6f 2yo winner (Group 3; RPR 102), is a half-sister to useful winners Hors De Combat (7f/1m) and Stroll Patrol (6f); appeals on paper and must enter calculations for powerful stable.
runners.comment: Showcasing colt; appeals on paper and must enter calculations for powerful stable
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Thai Ginger
runners.spotlight: Sent off 2-1 favourite but looked green when a never-dangerous fifth of seven in novice at Ripon (6f, good) on his debut 22 days ago; this No Nay Never colt looks capable of a fair bit better.
runners.comment: Green when never-dangerous debut fifth at Ripon; looks capable of a fair bit better

runners.name: Dakota Brave
runners.spotlight: 11-1, looked very much in need of the experience when sixth of seven in a 6f Lingfield novice on his debut 17 days ago; this son of Sioux Nation can take a step forward.
runners.comment: Green when sixth of seven at Lingfield on his debut; can take a step forward

races.race_name: Owenstown Stud Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Native Warrior
runners.spotlight: British raider rediscovered his best when a visor was added last June, winning three handicaps from five starts, the last being the valuable Challenge Cup (7f, soft) at Ascot; ran right up to form on his stakes debut in a Saint-Cloud Group 3 (1m, soft) for seasonal return when a close runner-up; entitled to improve for that and top rated.
runners.comment: British raider was a good second in a Gr.3 on return; entitled to improve and top rated

runners.name: Expanded
runners.spotlight: 2024 Dewhurst runner-up on soft; highly tried in a three-run campaign last season, which never really took off; seasonal return was much more positive when coming home strongly from off the pace in a Leopardstown Listed contest (1m, yielding to soft) to take the runner-up spot behind a better-fancied stablemate; big player but this better ground is a small concern.
runners.comment: Left moderate campaign behind when runner-up on seasonal return; big player

runners.name: King Cuan
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner at 6f-7f; won just once last season when landing a three-runner auction race at Fairyhouse (6f, good); subsequently placed in Listed and Group 3 company; built on decent Cork return when running on well for fourth over 5f here last time, from disadvantageous side of the track; this trip suits better and not ruled out.
runners.comment: Built on decent seasonal return when running on over 5f here last time; not ruled out

runners.name: Rahmi
runners.spotlight: Three-time h'cap winner, including over C&D in final 2024 start; ran just three times last season; produced a smart display to win a 7f premier handicap at the Irish Champions meeting; towards rear throughout on comeback in a 7f Curragh Listed contest (soft to heavy); may be better suited by this ground but Tashakour looks like the yard's main hope.
runners.comment: Three-time h'cap winner was disappointing on return; Tashakour looks stable's main hope
runners.owner: Richard McNally & Leo Walsh & L Murrin

runners.name: East Hampton
runners.spotlight: Sole win last season came in the C&D Madrid handicap; ran a career best when a close second in the Curragh Group 2 Minstrel Stakes (7f, good to yielding) behind subsequent Group 1 winner Diego Velazquez; highly encouraging second in a Curragh Listed contest on return (7f, soft to heavy); however, he runs well fresh so there might not be boundless improvement to come.
runners.comment: Encouraging second on return but runs well fresh, so might not be boundless improvement

runners.name: Carolina Jetstream
runners.spotlight: Boasts useful form at Dundalk, the scene of all four wins; turf has been less lucrative and was beaten 11.5l in a Curragh Group 3 last time (1m2f, soft); others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Boasts useful AW form but turf has been less lucrative; 11.5l behind in Gr.3 latest
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Greek Mythology
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Dundalk median auction race on debut in December; 3.25l second in a four-runner Gowran rated race on his return (7f, soft) last month; could step forward on this quicker ground.
runners.comment: Won AW debut in Dec'; okay run on return and could step forward on this better ground

runners.name: Greydreambeliever
runners.spotlight: Some useful form in Britain for Karl Burke before being bought by current connections for 30,000gns; made a good stable debut when a runner-up in a Gowran handicap and improved when filling the same spot dropped to 7f back there next time; those runs were on soft, while her British form is on good, so could improve, but this is a tall task; Greek Mythology is stable first-string.
runners.comment: Useful British form and positive h'cap runs for new yard but this is a tall task
runners.owner: K Blake/Mrs E Palmer/M Wilson/N Luck

runners.name: Wannabe Royal
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner over 7f for Joseph O'Brien on soft; ran well over that trip at the Curragh on seasonal/stable debut; poor over 6f at Cork last time; others more likely, especially on this ground.
runners.comment: Maiden winner for Joseph O'Brien was poor at Cork; others more likely

runners.name: Tashakour
runners.spotlight: Good second in a Navan maiden on seasonal return (1m, heavy); showed the benefit of that run when successful at Cork last time (1m, good to yielding); a bit to find in this company.
runners.comment: Cork m'den winner last time has a bit to find in this company
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Chantez
runners.spotlight: Listed winner at Leopardstown as a 2yo; best of four Group 3 runs last season was when third at Leopardstown in July (7f, good to firm); drops in grade but this looks hard all the same.
runners.comment: Listed 2yo winner was unable to bag a Gr.3 in four runs last term; drops in grade now

races.race_name: Sun's 'Save Our Bets' Handicap

runners.name: Cotai Lights
runners.spotlight: Landed 6f Newcastle novice in November and he's continued in good nick in handicaps this spring, cheekpieces on when fourth of 11 at Newmarket (7f) 15 days ago; in the mix once more.
runners.comment: In good nick, cheekpieces on when fourth at Newmarket 15 days ago; in the mix once more

runners.name: Horace Wallace
runners.spotlight: Cosily got off the mark in 6f Wolverhampton novice in February but he failed to kick on in handicaps, only seventh back there (5f) in March; since gelded and could still do better on his turf debut.
runners.comment: Failed to kick on in handicaps this season; since gelded and could still do better now

runners.name: Kody B
runners.spotlight: Signed off for 2025 with 6f maiden victory at Pontefract; went too freely in first-time tongue tie when beating just one home in 7f Newmarket handicap last month though; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Too free when beating one home in 7f Newmarket handicap last month; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Gouken
runners.spotlight: Placed on all his five runs at 2yrs before (gelded) getting off the mark in 6f Newcastle maiden on his return last month; he must enter calculations back in handicap company.
runners.comment: Off the mark in Newcastle maiden on return; must enter calculations back in a handicap

runners.name: Circle Of Trust
runners.spotlight: Signed off at 2yrs with an improved second in 7f Yarmouth nursery; raced on the unfavoured part of the track when tenth in 7f Leicester handicap on his return and shouldn't be written off in a change of headgear.
runners.comment: Raced on unfavoured part of track when tenth at Leicester on return; not written off
runners.owner: Mrs R G Hillen And Partner

runners.name: Mo Of Cairo
runners.spotlight: Showed fair winning form at 2yrs and he returned from eight months off with an encouraging sixth of 14 in 5f Thirsk handicap 29 days ago, keeping on late; not out of things with that run under his belt.
runners.comment: Encouraging sixth at Thirsk 29 days ago; not out of things with that run under his belt

runners.name: Daizen
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with breakthrough victory at Ayr (6f); off seven months and shaped as if the run was needed when a fading fifth of seven in 6f Pontefract handicap on his recent return; can take a step forward now.
runners.comment: Shaped as if run was needed when fifth at Pontefract on return; can take a step forward

runners.name: Alasrae
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed an excellent winter on the AW, scoring three times at 6f, and comes here on the back of a good third of ten in 6f Lingfield handicap 19 days ago when not enjoying the smoothest of passages; can play a prominent role again back on turf.
runners.comment: Enjoyed an excellent winter on the AW; can play a prominent role again back on turf

runners.name: Dandy Breeze
runners.spotlight: Progressive son of Dandy Man who got off the mark in 5f Newcastle novice in January and returned from over three months off with an excellent second of eight in 6f handicap there 16 days ago; likely capable of better still on this turf debut.
runners.comment: Excellent second at Newcastle 16 days ago; likely capable of better still on turf debut

runners.name: Marcellinus
runners.spotlight: Gelded/off five months before taking a step forward to make a winning start in handicaps at Bath (5.5f) last month; up 7lb but this son of Showcasing looks to have more to offer; likely player.
runners.comment: Winning start in handicaps at Bath; up 7lb but looks to have more to offer; likely player

runners.name: Red Spells Danger
runners.spotlight: Improving son of Cotai Glory who spreadeagled his field on his first go over 6f in a Ripon maiden nine days ago; a most interesting handicap debutant.
runners.comment: Spreadeagled his field on first go over 6f at Ripon; interesting handicap debutant

runners.name: Proof
runners.spotlight: Kickstarted 2026 with gutsy 5f Southwell success in April but suffered early interference when ninth in 5f Chester handicap nine days ago; it would come as no surprise to see him bounce back.
runners.comment: Suffered interference when ninth at Chester latest; no surprise to see him bounce back

runners.name: Mighty Magnus
runners.spotlight: A fair and consistent 5.5f scorer at 2yrs; back from six months off with a good clear second of 13 in 6f Ripon handicap last month; not taken lightly despite a 4lb weights rise.
runners.comment: Good clear second at Ripon last month; not taken lightly despite a 4lb weights rise

runners.name: Go Vince Go
runners.spotlight: A dual C&D winner in an excellent 2025 campaign; got right back on track when fourth of 12 in 6f Chester handicap 11 days ago; must enter calculations off the same mark.
runners.comment: A dual C&D winner; good fourth at Chester latest so must enter calculations off same mark

races.race_name: Coolmore Stud Irish EBF Fillies Sprint Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Velozee
runners.spotlight: Sioux Nation filly is 2-2; scored by a short head on soft at Cork on debut (5f); impressive display in four runner listed race at the Curragh latest (5f, good), after which the Albany Stakes was nominated as a possible target; bred to get 6f and sets the standard; yard in red hot form.
