runners.comment: 2-2, impressive 5f listed win latest, bred to get 6f, sets the standard for red hot yard

runners.name: Victorious
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett filly from an excellent family made a winning start in a C&D maiden, displaying a smart turn of foot to beat Aedhmar (who was only a neck behind Velozee on her previous start at Cork) by 1.75l; yard's runners often improve plenty for their first runs and she looks an exciting prospect; big chance.
runners.comment: Impressive win on debut over C&D, from excellent family, should improve, big chance

runners.name: Vollering
runners.spotlight: British raider; Sioux Nation filly displayed plenty of speed and ran on strongly in the final furlong to win a Redcar novice by 2.5l on debut (5f, gd-fm); the manner of that win and her pedigree suggests that 6f should pose no problems; looks well worth a go at this level and is no forlorn hope.
runners.comment: Powered clear late on to win on debut over 5f at Redcar, should get 6f, no forlorn hope

runners.name: Controlla
runners.spotlight: One of two for stable; Night Of Thunder filly; 160,000gns yearling; closely related to 8.6f AW 2yo/1m turf winner Khaloosy (RPR 114), half-sister to winners Moqtarreb (1m inc Listed; 109) and Zaajel (7f AW 2yo; 76); unplaced dam from family of Nathaniel; pitched in at the deep end on debut but yard's juveniles always respected.
runners.comment: 160,000gns Night Of Thunder filly, related to useful winners, pitched in at the deep end

runners.name: Maria Quiteria
runners.spotlight: Churchill filly justified skinny odds to win a five runner 5f Dundalk maiden decisively on debut in March; should stay 6f and handle quick ground on breeding but that form has taken some knocks and this demands a whole lot more.
runners.comment: Justified skinny odds on debut at Dundalk, should handle turf but this demands a lot more

runners.name: Dancing Destiny
runners.spotlight: Magna Grecia filly got off the mark on debut at Roscommon (7.5f, good) six days ago; didn't shape like she was crying out for a drop back in trip that day and her dam was best over 1m2f-1m4f; may be vulnerable to speedier rivals at this distance.
runners.comment: Debut win over 7.5f at Roscommon, not sure this drop back to 6f will suit

races.race_name: Sky Bet Extra Places Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Ride The Thunder
runners.spotlight: Won 1m2f Nottingham maiden last June; on the premises in all his three handicaps, closest last time when he came from the back at Haydock (1m6f, good) but to not quite such good effect in the end as Valiancy who made the same move on the other side of the track; gelded since and hooded first time for this reappearance; up 4lb but looks a serious contender.
runners.comment: 2nd to Valiancy final 3yo start; gelded since and hooded first time for this reappearance

runners.name: Valiancy
runners.spotlight: Made debut last May and all six runs were on good ground; maiden winner here who was increasingly competitive coming from behind in his three handicaps, winning in first-time cheekpieces from Ride The Thunder at Haydock (1m6f) in September when last seen; up 7lb, back slightly in trip and no headgear, but he's still one to note.
runners.comment: Won from Ride The Thunder at Haydock (1m6f) in September; up 7lb and headgear removed

runners.name: Gibside
runners.spotlight: Strong finish from the rear secured the Chester Plate (2m2f, good) last May off 1lb higher than today and he also won over 1m4f on soft at Catterick in October; needs to leave his three efforts this season a long way behind him, latest when not faring nearly so well with the draw back in Chester Plate, and others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Well handicapped but needs to leave his three efforts this season a long way behind him

runners.name: Artisan Dancer
runners.spotlight: Industrious and dependable sort over 1m6f-2m2f but his turf strike-rate is just 1-18 and return to turf last time saw him well held.
runners.comment: His turf strike-rate is just 1-18 and return to turf last time saw him well held

runners.name: Nolton Cross
runners.spotlight: Six wins (latest in February) from 24 AW starts but 0-10 on turf; Newmarket 15 days ago saw first turf race since 2023 and he came last of eight.
runners.comment: 0-10 on turf; Newmarket 15 days ago saw first turf run since 2023 and he was last of eight

runners.name: Savrola
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who was progressive last year (over 1m6f, 1m5f here and 2m) and finished either first or second in all five of those races, but he wasn't seen after July; a shorter break (178 days) didn't prevent a win first time out last term and he should be retained in serious calculations.
runners.comment: Front-runner; first or second in all five races last year but he wasn't seen after July

runners.name: Military Air
runners.spotlight: Useful in France, finishing third in a Listed race last season (1m4f, good to soft); changed hands for 45,000gns in October but well beaten on stable debut at Pontefract (1m2f; 8-1) last month when making the running in his usual hood; tongue tied last year but not this term; others look more solid.
runners.comment: Ex-French; well beaten on stable debut at Pontefract (1m2f) last month

runners.name: Trojan Sun
runners.spotlight: Often ridden by Lauren Young; 4-12 at Musselburgh, including three 1m6f/2m wins on good ground last May-October; also scored at Nottingham in October, a runaway win over 2m2f on heavy going which sees him 2lb higher today; returned to form when third over 2m at Musselburgh three weeks ago but this C&D makes him look vulnerable.
runners.comment: Back-to-form third at Musselburgh (2m) last time but this C&D makes him look vulnerable

runners.name: Mountain Road
runners.spotlight: 4-15 for David Simcock; absent for 582 days before having two runs for new yard this spring, runs which showed that significant ability remains but he needs to build on those performances.
runners.comment: 582 days off before two runs for new yard this spring; needs to build on those efforts

runners.name: Putapoundinthejar
runners.spotlight: Just five Flat races, faring easily best when he won a handicap at Bellewstown (1m3f, soft) last month; returned to hurdling for a respectable show at Punchestown festival 19 days ago; his trainer's three runners at Chester last week were all well held, albeit with two in the Chester Cup, but this one should have more to offer in this sphere and goes on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Won a handicap at Bellewstown (1m3f, soft) before a respectable hurdling effort; shortlist

runners.name: Spanish Hustle
runners.spotlight: Easily best known at Hamilton where he's 6-20, including two wins last summer; competitive if he returns in last season's peak form but he's needed at least his first run back in previous seasons.
runners.comment: Easily best known at Hamilton but needed at least his first run back in previous seasons

runners.name: Ghaiyya
runners.spotlight: Had three 1m4f wins last summer and better than ever when she took a six-runner race at Doncaster (1m4f, good to firm) in clearcut style on reappearance three weeks ago, leading over 1f out; up 5lb and she was well held in this higher grade last term, but she is in serious calculations nevertheless.
runners.comment: Better than ever with Doncaster win (1m4f, good to firm) in clearcut style on reappearance

races.race_name: Goffs Lacken Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Havana Anna
runners.spotlight: Maiden and Listed winner over 5f; beaten only a nose in a Group 3 over the minimum trip at Longchamp, though produced her best effort when chasing home True Love in the 6f Group 1 Cheveley Park; failed to cope with the challenge of the Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf Sprint; appealing given her top-rating and she's unpenalised but lacks a run.
runners.comment: Listed winner/Group 1 placed is top-rated and unpenalised but lacks a run

runners.name: Charles Darwin
runners.spotlight: Beaten on debut at Cork before going the rest of the season unbeaten; landed a Navan maiden (6f, soft), a four-runner conditions event here (5f, good) and ultimately the Norfolk Stakes at Royal Ascot (5f, good to firm); workmanlike when justifying short odds at Navan on seasonal return (6f, good to yielding) and there is likely improvement to come; holds Group 1 entries for Ascot.
runners.comment: Last season's Norfolk Stakes winner was workmanlike on return but improvement likely

runners.name: Royal Bay Cen
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f French winner at two; good efforts in a pair of Group 3s before signing off her French career with an underwhelming Group 2 start; mightily impressive Irish/stable debut at Cork (5.5f, soft) when pulverising seven rivals by 9l; big chance if building on that on this better ground.
runners.comment: Mighty impressive Irish/stable debut in a Cork Listed race; big chance if handling ground

runners.name: Josh's Joy
runners.spotlight: Positive efforts in maidens before landing a Naas contest at the fourth attempt (6f, yielding); okay return in a Curragh handicap last time when a 4l seventh (6f, soft to heavy); this is a big ask.
runners.comment: Won her m'den at the fourth attempt last season; okay return but this is a big ask

runners.name: Oh Cecelia
runners.spotlight: Cork maiden winner (5f, good) for Jack Davison last year; made a winning stable debut in a Naas handicap in March (5f, soft) and added another victory in a Cork rated contest (5f, soft); beaten 9.5l by Royal Bay Cen in a Cork Listed race last time (5.5f, soft); a lot to find.
runners.comment: Beaten 9.5l by Royal Bay Cen in a Cork Listed race last time; a lot to find

runners.name: Kindergarten
runners.spotlight: Winner of her sole start last year at Navan (6f, yielding); disputing second in a Navan Listed contest before fading late into fourth (6f, good to yielding); plausible improvement to come from that first run back and was just 1.75l behind the winner Charles Darwin, although worse off at the weights now.
runners.comment: Plausible improvement to come over 1.75l defeat behind Charles Darwin on just second start

runners.name: Tahcawin
runners.spotlight: Easily won a 6f Fairyhouse nursery last season; built on a positive comeback against older rivals at Gowran to run a career best when filling the second spot in a Naas handicap eight days ago (6f, good); she's progressing, but looks out of her depth here.
runners.comment: Ran a career best last time when second in a h'cap but looks out of her depth here

runners.name: Chicago Call
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f Fairyhouse maiden over this trip last July (good); best form when just denied in a Listed event at the same track in September (6f, yielding); improved over moderate seasonal return at Cork when a 3l sixth at Navan last time (6f, good to yielding); others preferred.
runners.comment: Improved over moderate seasonal return when 3l behind at Navan latest; others preferred
runners.owner: Samuel M Mencoff & Anthony Paul Smurfit

races.race_name: Sky Bet Build A Bet Handicap

runners.name: Station X
runners.spotlight: Turf wins as a 2yo came on soft/heavy ground and the midweek rain will have helped, although he'd have preferred more; highly consistent but a 2lb rise for his recent AW win puts him on a career-high mark into a more competitive handicap, down from 6f.
runners.comment: 2lb rise puts him on a career-high mark into a more competitive handicap, down from 6f

runners.name: Mon Na Slieve
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent front-runner who's given a third try in a tongue-tie following a below-par effort back on turf last time (made much of the running from the rail draw at Musselburgh); on a competitive mark if he's on a going day though.
runners.comment: Inconsistent front-runner; on a competitive mark if he's on a going day though

runners.name: Pal Joey
runners.spotlight: Found a Newcastle handicap run to suit when winning over this trip in January but has otherwise done most racing over further; the early impact of the visor may have worn off now and he was inclined to pull hard when well held back on turf a month ago.
runners.comment: Has done most racing over further; the early impact of the visor may have worn off now

runners.name: Azuinthejungle
runners.spotlight: Progressive in lesser company last summer, when completing a hat-trick, latterly off 6lb lower; excuses subsequently, including when getting upset in the stalls (not for the first time) on his comeback behind two of these three weeks ago.
runners.comment: Progressive in lesser company last summer; can have issues at the stalls; needs better
runners.owner: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde

runners.name: Little Mi Mi
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish mare who's won three this year, proving at Ayr last time that she can do it on turf as well as Tapeta; upped another 5lb, though, and this is the first time she's run in higher than 0-65 company; not ruled out but she'll find this opposition tougher.
runners.comment: Three wins this year, including last time, but has first run in higher than 0-65 company
runners.owner: N Irving, F Steele & I Jardine

runners.name: Alpha Magic
runners.spotlight: His two wins came on good to soft ground over 6f, including here off this mark last June; entitled to have needed his recent comeback from nine months off, when leading for a long way, and he's low mileage compared to most of these; first run over 5f.
runners.comment: Entitled to have needed his recent comeback from nine months off; first run over 5f

runners.name: Vantheman
runners.spotlight: Patchy last season and more of the same this term, failing to build on a Tapeta win off 3lb lower in three runs since; latest effort in first-time blinkers is readily ignored but it's hard to know what to expect for a yard also responsible for Mon Na Slieve.
runners.comment: Patchy last season and more of the same this term; hard to know what to expect

runners.name: Woohoo
runners.spotlight: Won four times early last summer, including over C&D, and climbed 19lb up the weights; made it three wins and a second in four starts under Lauren Young when turning in a career-best effort to score at Musselburgh last time out and may well be up to defying a 3lb rise.
runners.comment: Won four early last summer, including over C&D, and again at Musselburgh on latest start

runners.name: Master Of My Fate
runners.spotlight: Most wins have come on the AW but he is fine on turf (2-11) and turned in another in-form effort at Nottingham three weeks ago; there's little wiggle room off this mark (3lb above his highest successful rating) and he'll need a career best if he's to win.
runners.comment: In good form; little wiggle room off this mark and he'll need a career best if he's to win
runners.owner: Ms Janelle Cao, Ross Harmon & Partner

runners.name: Marty Hopkirk
runners.spotlight: Looked flattered from the front in a small field on his handicap debut last July, since when he's struggled; well held on his Southwell comeback (won his novice on Tapeta) early last month and may need some help from the handicapper.
runners.comment: Well held on his AW comeback early last month and may need some help from the handicapper

runners.name: Vince L'Amour
runners.spotlight: Wins have all come over easy tracks in the spring/autumn on good or softer ground; won well at Ripon last weekend but was always well positioned from the rail draw and a 6lb rise looks plenty as he goes back up in class.
runners.comment: Won from the rail draw at Ripon and a 6lb rise looks plenty as he goes back up in class

runners.name: Reigning Profit
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who boasts a good record at this time of year; will always be reliant on some luck given his hold-up style, which he hasn't had this year; on a handy mark when things do fall his way again, though, and is one to consider.
runners.comment: C&D winner who boasts a good record at this time of year; not much luck lately; player

runners.name: Spring Is Sprung
runners.spotlight: As good as ever on Tapeta lately, albeit turned over as favourite on last two outings; returns to turf 3lb above his highest winning mark and at the age of seven (no surprise to see Rhys Elliott up to take off 5lb) and he will need to get things bang on.
runners.comment: As good as ever on Tapeta lately; returns to turf 3lb above his highest winning mark

runners.name: Manila Scouse
runners.spotlight: May have needed his recent comeback from six months off at his age (cracked late on having raced prominently from a low stall over 6f round Chester); 2lb below his last successful mark and has won here before but David Allan has opted for Vince L'Amour.
runners.comment: 2lb below his last successful mark and has won here before; David Allan on Vince L'Amour

runners.name: Jordan Electrics
runners.spotlight: Nine course wins to his name from 22 starts here (3-7 over C&D); in good form of late, having spent last year in the grip of the handicapper, but that was over 7f and whether he still has the toe for 5f, at his age off this mark, is the question; yard also runs Woohoo.
runners.comment: Nine course wins; whether he has the toe for 5f, at his age off this mark, is the question
runners.owner: B Jordan, Brian Jordan, S Jordan & N McConnell

races.race_name: Sole Power Sprint Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Mission Central
runners.spotlight: Won three of his six starts as a juvenile, including a Group 3 at the Curragh (6f, good to yielding) and a valuable conditions race at Ascot (6f, good); struggled when dropped to 5f at the Breeders' Cup on final start; ran out a ready winner of a C&D Listed contest on seasonal return to mark his first victory over the minimum trip; carries a penalty but big chance of following up.
runners.comment: Ready Listed C&D winner on seasonal return and big chance of following up under penalty

runners.name: Lady Iman
runners.spotlight: Amassed an impressive CV in her opening runs last season; won her AW maiden before landing a 5f Curragh Listed contest, and a Naas Group 3; failed to justify 8-15 odds in a Curragh Group 2 before resuming progress with a Molecomb Stakes victory at Goodwood; form tapered off in her final two Group runs; big player on ratings but lacks a run.
runners.comment: Impressive CV in opening runs last term; big player on ratings but lacks a run

runners.name: Likedbymike
runners.spotlight: 1-12 on turf but has had a few near misses, including when beaten a short head in a 5f Curragh handicap (soft) on penultimate; ran a career best over C&D last time when 0.75l behind Mission Central; better off with that rival now; good chance with a repeat.
runners.comment: 1-12 but some near misses inc' when 0.75l behind Mission Central here latest; chance
runners.owner: Mrs Michael J Byrne & Jerry P Murphy

runners.name: Kerdos
runners.spotlight: Classy sprinter for Clive Cox and won a Listed and a Group 2 for that yard; decent run on debut for this yard at Dundalk before disappointing in Cork Listed race next time on soft; much better over C&D last time when a 2.75l sixth behind Mission Central.
runners.comment: Listed and Gr.2 winner was a 2.75l sixth behind Mission Central last time over C&D
runners.owner: Michael Scaife/Liam Ferguson/Simon Murphy/H S Khunkhuna

runners.name: Keke
runners.spotlight: Seven wins over 5f and 6f generally on good ground; won off 97 in a 6f handicap here last time (good); his mark of 104 is a career high but he will need to improve again now entering stakes company.
runners.comment: Added seventh win when winning h'cap here last time; needs to improve again

runners.name: Valiant Force
runners.spotlight: Sole win on turf in the 2023 Norfolk Stakes; has won five conditions races at Dundalk since, the most recent a 4.25l win over 5f in February; well beaten at Lingfield, Newcastle and when 7.5l behind Keke in a 6f handicap here last time; others arrive in better form.
runners.comment: Just 1-12 on turf and 7.5l behind Keke in a h'cap last time; others arrive in better form
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Ipanema Queen
runners.spotlight: Winner of a 6f Curragh maiden but seemed better suited to 5f as the season progressed; won two Listed races at the Curragh and Dundalk over the minimum trip; narrowly lost out to longer priced stablemate on seasonal debut at Dundalk; 5-4 favourite at Cork (5.5f, soft) last time when a disappointing seventh of eight; this ground may suit better but others preferred.
runners.comment: Won a m'den and two Listed races last season but a disappointing seventh at Cork last time
runners.owner: Stonestreet Stables, LLC & AMO Racing Limited

runners.name: Gazelle d'Or
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner at 5f-6f on good and yielding ground; not at best on Curragh return; didn't get the clearest of runs when 12th of 15 and 5.5l behind the winner Mission Central in a C&D Listed contest last time; others preferred.
runners.comment: Not the clearest of runs when 12th of 15 in C&D Listed contest latest; others preferred

runners.name: Sarahmae
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner over this trip on varying ground types but hasn't been at her best so far this season and this looks too tough.
runners.comment: Three-time winner hasn't been at her best so far this season and this is tough
runners.owner: Mary B Hogan & Michael M O'Brien

runners.name: Billie Be Quick
runners.spotlight: Won her third handicap off 77 at Chepstow last August (5f, good to soft); no real impact in Listed races since and well beaten back in a handicap here last time (6f, good); others more appealing.
runners.comment: No real impact since winning h'cap off 77 last Aug'; others more appealing

runners.name: Black Forza
runners.spotlight: Winner of a Goodwood Group 2 in 2024; sparingly raced last year, well beaten in a Curragh Group 3 (6f, good); some better efforts twice at Dundalk but more needed here and lacks a run.
runners.comment: Gr.2 winner in 2024 ran okay on AW in final two runs; more needed here and lacks a run

runners.name: Moss Tucker
runners.spotlight: Former Flying Five winner reached a peak rating of 114 before losing his form in the latter half of 2024; missed all of last season; return in a C&D Listed contest last time was not totally devoid of promise when 4.75l behind Mission Central; may build on that.
runners.comment: Gr.1 winner in 2014 missed all of last season; some promise in his return here last time

races.race_name: Sky Bet Price Boosts Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Epidavros
runners.spotlight: On a losing run but this C&D scorer comes here in decent nick, fourth of 12 in 1m1f Musselburgh handicap three weeks ago; 2lb out of the handicap here though.
runners.comment: C&D scorer; in decent nick, fourth at Musselburgh latest; 2lb out of handicap though

runners.name: Seren Star
runners.spotlight: Progressed well in 2025 (a three-time winner around 1m) until hooded and only 13th in Saint-Cloud Listed event (1m) on her final run in November; headgear is quickly dispensed with and she appeals as the sort to do well again in 2026.
runners.comment: Progressed well in 2025 until 13th at Saint-Cloud; appeals as the sort to do well in 2026

runners.name: Plaid
runners.spotlight: Has improved a chunk with each of her three runs, landing 1m Kempton novice in December before returning with a neck second of six in 1m handicap there in March; big shout back from a break nudged up just 1lb.
runners.comment: Improving filly; second in Kempton handicap in March; big shout nudged up 1lb

runners.name: Dunkeld Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways at Haydock (1m) in April and she backed it up with a good fourth of nine in 7.5f Beverley handicap 13 days ago; in the mix off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Good fourth in Beverley handicap 13 days ago; in the mix off an unchanged mark

runners.name: Cancelled
runners.spotlight: A fair 9.5f winner in Ireland; got back on track for her current yard with third of eight in 1m2f Haydock handicap 22 days ago; needs considering off an easing mark.
runners.comment: Back on track for current yard with Haydock third 22 days ago; needs considering

runners.name: Second Fiddle
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a superb 2025 when a six-time winner (including here) but yet to fire this season, beating just one home in a 1m Musselburgh handicap last month; she's 4lb out of the handicap too.
runners.comment: Enjoyed a superb 2025 when a six-time winner (including here) but yet to fire this season

runners.name: Azahara Palace
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m4f scorer for Hughie Morrison who showed winning hurdling form over the winter for her current yard; not out of things back in this sphere with a visor added.
runners.comment: In winning hurdling form over winter for current yard; not out of things back on Flat

runners.name: Crystal Flyer
runners.spotlight: Consistent for Tom Ward in 2025; changed hands for 70,000gns but she went too freely when beating only one home on her stable debut in a 1m4f Listed event at Goodwood on her recent return; she's the sort to bounce back.
runners.comment: Too free when beating one home on stable debut at Goodwood; she's the sort to bounce back
runners.owner: Castle Racing Co Ltd & Mps Racing Ltd

races.race_name: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap

runners.name: The Good Biscuit
runners.spotlight: Closed out last season with a pair of soft-ground wins and has resumed in good form off his revised mark, latterly when having two of these behind at Chester last weekend; adaptable as regards ground and tactics, and can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Resumed in good form off his revised mark; adaptable and can't be discounted

runners.name: Jonny Concrete
runners.spotlight: Stays 1m and two of his three wins came over 7f but a good test over this trip is fine; at his best last year around this time and resumed with a solid effort as favourite at Chester nine days ago (behind The Good Biscuit); will rightly be popular.
runners.comment: A good test over this trip is fine; at his best last year around this time; player

runners.name: Lion's House
runners.spotlight: Wins have all come on Tapeta and does most racing now at Newcastle; decent second there last time, back up from 5f, but this is stronger and he's far from certain to repeat the effort.
runners.comment: Decent second on the AW last time, but he's far from certain to repeat the effort

runners.name: Stirrup Cup
runners.spotlight: Soft-ground C&D winner last autumn who's twice been unlucky in big-field Thirsk handicaps switched to cheekpieces this spring; better off with Juan Les Pins than on his comeback and enters calculations.
runners.comment: Twice been unlucky in big-field Thirsk handicaps this spring; enters calculations

runners.name: Water Of Leith
runners.spotlight: Prolific winner down the years but has his quirks and is hard to catch right now; the return to 6f won't hurt and he's quite capable if everything falls right, but Montezuma looks his trainer's best chance (also runs Abduction).
runners.comment: Prolific winner down the years but has his quirks and is hard to catch right now
runners.owner: Adam Mills And Jonnie Delta Racing

runners.name: Montezuma
runners.spotlight: Three solid efforts over 5f at Musselburgh this spring; no worries about the return to 6f in another big field and he may yet be capable of better; high on the shortlist.
runners.comment: No worries about the return to 6f in another big field and may yet be capable of better
runners.owner: B Jordan, Brian Jordan, S Jordan & N McConnell

runners.name: Rock Opera
runners.spotlight: Winless in two years but has slid to a handy mark and resumed in decent form; holds each-way claims but this is a stronger race than the Thirsk handicap in which he was a bit unlucky not to finish closer last time.
runners.comment: Resumed in decent form; holds each-way claims but this is a stronger race than last time

runners.name: Juan Les Pins
runners.spotlight: Not as good now but enjoying a highly consistent spring, finishing a clear second off a 2lb lower mark at Ascot last weekend; no obvious reason he won't go well once more.
runners.comment: Not as good now but enjoying a consistent spring; no obvious reason he won't go well again

runners.name: Abduction
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D off this mark last summer but does most racing now over further and wants a good test at this trip; said to have run flat last time but does have his good and bad days now for a yard responsible for two others; 2-4 for Kaiya Fraser.
runners.comment: Wants a good test at this trip; said to have run flat last time; 2-4 for Kaiya Fraser

runners.name: Aberama Gold
runners.spotlight: Getting on but retains a lot of his old ability, is still consistent enough and has won over C&D before; rare off-day at Thirsk a fortnight ago, having beating a subsequent winner in a small field off 1lb lower previously; quite capable of bouncing back.
runners.comment: Had a rare off-day at Thirsk a fortnight ago but is quite capable of bouncing back

runners.name: Who Wants Me
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden who returns from seven months off having undergone a second round of wind surgery; done a lot of her racing over 7f and she'd be a surprise winner for a yard that has more obvious claims with Jonny Concrete.
runners.comment: Regressive maiden who returns from seven months off; would be a surprise winner

runners.name: Top Juggler
runners.spotlight: Fair record here for the Quinn yard; made a successful debut for his current trainer off 2lb lower last September but will need to be spot on as he returns from more than six months off.
runners.comment: Fair record here for the Quinn yard; will need to be spot on back from six months off

runners.name: Secret Guest
runners.spotlight: Only won one of his last 28 starts but has slid to a competitive mark as a result and was unlucky not to finish closer behind The Good Biscuit and Jonny Concrete at Chester (hampered late); holds each-way claims.
runners.comment: Unlucky not to finish closer behind two of these at Chester; holds each-way claims

runners.name: Mae Amor
runners.spotlight: Struggled in three runs after joining from David O'Meara last autumn and resumes from five months off 2lb above her last winning mark; bit to overcome against some in-form, race-fit rivals.
runners.comment: Resumes from five months off 2lb above her last winning mark; bit to overcome

runners.name: Beauty Choice
runners.spotlight: Winless since 2024 and winless in sprints since 2023; said to have hung left when well beaten on his return to turf seven weeks ago and only holds outside claims.
runners.comment: Said to have hung left when well beaten on his return to turf seven weeks ago; outsider

races.race_name: Flutter Proud To Support Young Lives Vs Cancer Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Krissy
runners.spotlight: There was an extraordinary turnaround in her fortunes late last year and she was better still when winning from the front in a 12-runner race at Musselburgh (1m1f, good) on return to turf three weeks ago; with earlier AW wins over 1m2f and 1m4f, this step back up in trip does not look a worry and going up another 4lb may also be manageable; major player.
runners.comment: 1m2f/1m4f AW wins and better still when scoring over 1m1f at Musselburgh three weeks ago

runners.name: Jaminoz
runners.spotlight: 4-27 at Hamilton, which has dominated his itinerary but he also has two wins elsewhere; made a respectable return to Flat over C&D two weeks ago under Lexie Clarkson and could build on that to get seriously involved.
runners.comment: 4-27 at Hamilton; respectable return to Flat over C&D two weeks ago under Lexie Clarkson

runners.name: Letsbefrank
runners.spotlight: Three wins (C&D, 1m6f and 2m1f) in summer 2024 and, from a reduced mark, back on the scoresheet  at Musselburgh (1m4f) three weeks ago when arriving very late on the scene at 20-1 under Lauren Young to deny Fast Fred; back up 4lb but could take another step back up the ladder.
runners.comment: Very late on the scene at 20-1 to deny Fast Fred at Musselburgh (1m4f); player back up 4lb

runners.name: Fast Fred
runners.spotlight: Dependable sort who's been in career-best form with a win and two seconds this season; raised another 2lb but can show up well again after his two big runs at Musselburgh (pipped by Letsbefrank latest); highly respected.
runners.comment: Can show up well again after his two big runs at Musselburgh, pipped by Letsbefrank latest

runners.name: Sweet Kiss
runners.spotlight: Won three times over 1m2f under 7lb claimer Jake Dickson last August/September; progressive overall in that campaign and needs respect if she's sufficiently tuned up for this reappearance; market may guide.
runners.comment: Three wins last August/September; needs respect if tuned up for this reappearance

runners.name: Sure And Stedfast
runners.spotlight: Can get a bit worked up and not that consistent but he still won last summer at Ayr (1m2f) and Carlisle (1m1f) and ran creditably over 1m4f on final outing; no show at 33-1 for recent reappearance when a hood was enlisted, which it isn't today; competitive if this is one of his good days.
runners.comment: Not that consistent but won twice last summer; no show at 33-1 for recent return in a hood

runners.name: L'Eagle Aid
runners.spotlight: Usually races prominently but came from the rear, having fluffed the start, for one of his two 1m4f wins last summer; he'll need better than he's produced this season but is back to his last winning mark.
runners.comment: He'll need better than he's produced this season but is back to his last winning mark

runners.name: Furhaan
runners.spotlight: Won handicap debut at Windsor (heavy) in September and creditable fifth at Yarmouth (good) next time, both over an extended 1m3f; left Ed Walker for 32,000gns later in October and behind on both runs (1m4f; 25-1 and 28-1) this season, so seems best watched for now.
runners.comment: Behind on both runs (1m4f; 25-1 and 28-1) for new yard, so seems best watched for now

runners.name: Izzari
runners.spotlight: Raced at 1m this season, for a good fourth on reappearance but well-beaten second favourite here next time; this is a rethink on trip but a C&D third last June and 1m2f win last September are encouraging.
runners.comment: Rethink on trip but a C&D third last June and 1m2f win last September are encouraging

runners.name: Polygram
runners.spotlight: Went close more than once for Harry Charlton but left that yard an eight-race maiden and she's not threatened to put that right in three runs for new yard; has tried cheekpieces and now has tongue tied for step up in trip.
runners.comment: 11-race maiden; has tried cheekpieces and now has tongue tied for step up in trip

runners.name: Secret Force
runners.spotlight: 1m3f Flat and 2m hurdle wins last autumn on first two runs for Gavin Cromwell but pulled up one month ago on handicap hurdle debut when wearing tongue-strap and cheekpieces for the first time; the latter are not retained on this first run for new yard (same owner); could have more to offer on Flat.
runners.comment: 1m3f Flat and 2m hurdle wins initially for G Cromwell; could have more to offer on Flat

runners.name: Kisiyra
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden when trained by Johnny Murtagh in Ireland, placed twice as a 2yo; sold for 3,000euros in November 2024 but had only one run for current yard, when fourth of six at Thirsk (1m4f) last June; not beaten far that day but others bring more solid claims.
runners.comment: 0-7 in Ireland and 4th of six last June on her only start for current yard; none too solid

races.race_name: Preakness Stakes (Grade 1) (Dirt)

runners.name: Ocelli
runners.spotlight: Late-running maiden, much improved with blinkers removed last two starts; ran a huge race when 70-1 for Kentucky Derby last time, making a big move around home turn and looking the likely winner in straight until edging left and tiring, still finishing just 1l third to Golden Tempo; tough to beat if reproducing that on fourth start in nine weeks (also needs clear run from inside and another pace collapse) and must enter considerations.
runners.comment: Maiden; came from behind when 70-1 third in Kentucky Derby; good chance with clear run
runners.owner: Ashley Durr, Anthony Tate & Front Page Equestrian LLC

runners.name: Taj Mahal
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten in three, all here in 2026, including black-type Miracle Wood (1m, muddy; by neck) In February and Federico Tesio (1m1f) in April; in the latter he set torrid pace, opened up again when pressed turning in and stayed on strongly to win by 8l; no winner of that event has followed up in this since 1983, but this colt has powerful owners, good looks, a good pedigree and lots of stamina; should get run of race sitting behind Napoleon Solo and surely must go well.
runners.comment: Unbeaten in three here, including black-type events by neck and 8l; strong claims
runners.owner: SF Racing LLC, Starlight Racing Et Al

runners.name: Incredibolt
runners.spotlight: Progressive juvenile in 2025, winning Grade 3 Street Sense at Churchill Downs; impressive winner of black-type Virginia Derby (1m1f; stayed on strongly and surged clear last furlong, Ocelli beaten 7l in sixth) at Colonial Downs in March, then good 4l sixth to Golden Tempo in Kentucky Derby, staying on dourly having raced closer to strong pace than first three finishers; thriving and one of the few with no serious stamina questions.
runners.comment: Progressive colt, staying-on 4l sixth in Kentucky Derby; should give another solid account

runners.name: Chip Honcho
runners.spotlight: Best when able to dominate (pulls hard when taken on) as when second, pair clear of Golden Tempo, to unbeaten Paladin in Grade 2 Risen Star at Fair Grounds in February; rested since fifth in Grade 2 Louisiana Derby (9.5f) there last time and reportedly training well; Jose Ortiz resumes the mount (rode him to maiden score) but claims rest on whether he settles better.
runners.comment: Grade 2 second to Paladin in February is good form; pulled too hard latest; claims
runners.owner: Leland Ackerley Racing LLC, James Sherwood et al

runners.name: Iron Honor
runners.spotlight: Game winner (first two clear) of Grade 3 Gotham at Aqueduct in February; drawn widest and bumped first turn when well beaten in Grade 2 Wood Memorial (1m1f) on same track last time, racing too freely and weakening; trainer Chad Brown (two Preakness winners) takes blinkers off and engages Flavien Prat; progressing well before the Wood and likely to do a lot better this time; dangerous to all.
runners.comment: Good Grade 3 winner in February; drawn wide and bumped in Wood Memorial; dangerous
runners.owner: St Elias Stable, William H Lawrence & Glassman Racing

runners.name: Napoleon Solo
runners.spotlight: Impressive winner of Grade 1 Champagne at Aqueduct as juvenile; foot issues since and only fifth in Grade 2 events at Gulfstream and Aqueduct (Wood Memorial) in 2026, albeit setting too strong a pace last time and still beaten under 3l; reportedly training brilliantly and will make bold bid from front.
runners.comment: Grade 1 winner as juvenile but only fifth in two Grade 2s this year; could bounce back

runners.name: Robusta
runners.spotlight: Easily best effort in six-race career when head second to Potente in Grade 2 San Felipe at Santa Anita in March; disappointing since, including from widest draw in Kentucky Derby when never able to challenge, plodding on and always towards rear; hard to support with any confidence.
runners.comment: Touched off in Grade 2 in March but well beaten twice since, including in Kentucky Derby

runners.name: The Hell We Did
runners.spotlight: Promising efforts in sprints on his opening three starts, notably when impressive allowance winner at Sunland Park in March; first race at 8.5f (bred to stay) when fair 2l second in Grade 3 Lexington at Keeneland last time, staying on steadily but swept aside by longshot winner; needs more.
runners.comment: Fair 2l second in Grade 3 at Keeneland (first go at 8.5f) but this is a much tougher test

runners.name: Crupper
runners.spotlight: Son of top-class stallion Candy Ride and improving, but needs to step on hugely on his 1m1f black-type win at Oaklawn Park in April, when he got home gamely in blanket finish; victory today would be a sizeable shock, even in an open year, but he could improve for the increased test of stamina.
runners.comment: Got home in blanket finish to black-type event (1m1f) at Oaklawn Park; outsider

runners.name: Talkin
runners.spotlight: Good winner of Saratoga maiden before second (6l behind Napoleon Solo) in Grade 1 Champagne at Aqueduct as 2yo; has not progressed, though respectable 13l third in Grade 1 Blue Grass Stakes at Keeneland latest start; hard to make top choice, even with Irad Ortiz in the plate.
runners.comment: Gets Irad Ortiz but needs improvement on 13l third in Grade 1 at Keeneland latest
runners.owner: Reeves Thoroughbred Racing, Pine Racing Stables Et Al

runners.name: Bull By The Horns
runners.spotlight: Well held in Grade 2 Fountain Of Youth Stakes at Gulfstream before winning 8.5f black-type event on Tapeta at Turfway Park by half a length (pace collapse, came from 20l off pace); that form leaves him with lots to find, but he is at least progressing nicely; will get far behind.
runners.comment: Narrow winner of black-type event on Tapeta surface at Turfway; needs to find a lot more

runners.name: Great White
runners.spotlight: Narrow winner of Listed race on Tapeta at Turfway Park, then well-beaten fifth in Grade 1 Blue Grass at Keeneland; has to prove he acts on dirt and he was withdrawn before start of Kentucky Derby after rearing and falling in starting-gate area.
runners.comment: Listed winner on Tapeta surface but no comparable form on dirt; has to prove he belongs

runners.name: Pretty Boy Miah
runners.spotlight: Speedy type who has impressed over shorter in maiden (6.5f) and allowance race (1m) at Aqueduct; talented, but unlikely to prove suited by step up in trip and he's drawn widest.
runners.comment: Stiff task after maiden (6.5f) and allowance (1m) wins at Aqueduct over shorter; wide draw
runners.owner: Team Penney Racing, Echo Racing Et Al

runners.name: Corona De Oro
runners.spotlight: Likeable maiden winner at Fair Grounds (1m) before third in Grade 3 Lexington at Keeneland last time, less than 1l behind The Hell We Did but finishing weakly; did not seem to relish that step up to 8.5f and has questions to answer over another furlong here.
runners.comment: Third in Grade 3 Lexington (8.5f) last time when finished weakly; stamina/class questions
runners.owner: On Our Own Stable LLC, Commonwealth Stable Et Al

races.race_name: Victoria Mile (Grade 1) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)
Owenstown Stud Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Native Warrior
runners.spotlight: British raider rediscovered his best when a visor was added last June, winning three handicaps from five starts, the last being the valuable Challenge Cup (7f, soft) at Ascot; ran right up to form on his stakes debut in a Saint-Cloud Group 3 (1m, soft) for seasonal return when a close runner-up; entitled to improve for that and top rated.
runners.comment: British raider was a good second in a Gr.3 on return; entitled to improve and top rated

runners.name: Expanded
runners.spotlight: 2024 Dewhurst runner-up on soft; highly tried in a three-run campaign last season, which never really took off; seasonal return was much more positive when coming home strongly from off the pace in a Leopardstown Listed contest (1m, yielding to soft) to take the runner-up spot behind a better-fancied stablemate; big player but this better ground is a small concern.
runners.comment: Left moderate campaign behind when runner-up on seasonal return; big player

runners.name: King Cuan
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner at 6f-7f; won just once last season when landing a three-runner auction race at Fairyhouse (6f, good); subsequently placed in Listed and Group 3 company; built on decent Cork return when running on well for fourth over 5f here last time, from disadvantageous side of the track; this trip suits better and not ruled out.
runners.comment: Built on decent seasonal return when running on over 5f here last time; not ruled out

runners.name: Rahmi
runners.spotlight: Three-time h'cap winner, including over C&D in final 2024 start; ran just three times last season; produced a smart display to win a 7f premier handicap at the Irish Champions meeting; towards rear throughout on comeback in a 7f Curragh Listed contest (soft to heavy); may be better suited by this ground but Tashakour looks like the yard's main hope.
runners.comment: Three-time h'cap winner was disappointing on return; Tashakour looks stable's main hope
runners.owner: Richard McNally & Leo Walsh & L Murrin

runners.name: East Hampton
runners.spotlight: Sole win last season came in the C&D Madrid handicap; ran a career best when a close second in the Curragh Group 2 Minstrel Stakes (7f, good to yielding) behind subsequent Group 1 winner Diego Velazquez; highly encouraging second in a Curragh Listed contest on return (7f, soft to heavy); however, he runs well fresh so there might not be boundless improvement to come.
runners.comment: Encouraging second on return but runs well fresh, so might not be boundless improvement

runners.name: Carolina Jetstream
runners.spotlight: Boasts useful form at Dundalk, the scene of all four wins; turf has been less lucrative and was beaten 11.5l in a Curragh Group 3 last time (1m2f, soft); others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Boasts useful AW form but turf has been less lucrative; 11.5l behind in Gr.3 latest
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Greek Mythology
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Dundalk median auction race on debut in December; 3.25l second in a four-runner Gowran rated race on his return (7f, soft) last month; could step forward on this quicker ground.
runners.comment: Won AW debut in Dec'; okay run on return and could step forward on this better ground

runners.name: Greydreambeliever
runners.spotlight: Some useful form in Britain for Karl Burke before being bought by current connections for 30,000gns; made a good stable debut when a runner-up in a Gowran handicap and improved when filling the same spot dropped to 7f back there next time; those runs were on soft, while her British form is on good, so could improve, but this is a tall task; Greek Mythology is stable first-string.
runners.comment: Useful British form and positive h'cap runs for new yard but this is a tall task
runners.owner: K Blake/Mrs E Palmer/M Wilson/N Luck

runners.name: Wannabe Royal
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner over 7f for Joseph O'Brien on soft; ran well over that trip at the Curragh on seasonal/stable debut; poor over 6f at Cork last time; others more likely, especially on this ground.
runners.comment: Maiden winner for Joseph O'Brien was poor at Cork; others more likely

runners.name: Tashakour
runners.spotlight: Good second in a Navan maiden on seasonal return (1m, heavy); showed the benefit of that run when successful at Cork last time (1m, good to yielding); a bit to find in this company.
runners.comment: Cork m'den winner last time has a bit to find in this company
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Chantez
runners.spotlight: Listed winner at Leopardstown as a 2yo; best of four Group 3 runs last season was when third at Leopardstown in July (7f, good to firm); drops in grade but this looks hard all the same.
runners.comment: Listed 2yo winner was unable to bag a Gr.3 in four runs last term; drops in grade now

races.race_name: Coolmore Stud Irish EBF Fillies Sprint Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Velozee
runners.spotlight: Sioux Nation filly is 2-2; scored by a short head on soft at Cork on debut (5f); impressive display in four runner listed race at the Curragh latest (5f, good), after which the Albany Stakes was nominated as a possible target; bred to get 6f and sets the standard; yard in red hot form.
runners.comment: 2-2, impressive 5f listed win latest, bred to get 6f, sets the standard for red hot yard

runners.name: Victorious
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett filly from an excellent family made a winning start in a C&D maiden, displaying a smart turn of foot to beat Aedhmar (who was only a neck behind Velozee on her previous start at Cork) by 1.75l; yard's runners often improve plenty for their first runs and she looks an exciting prospect; big chance.
runners.comment: Impressive win on debut over C&D, from excellent family, should improve, big chance

runners.name: Vollering
runners.spotlight: British raider; Sioux Nation filly displayed plenty of speed and ran on strongly in the final furlong to win a Redcar novice by 2.5l on debut (5f, gd-fm); the manner of that win and her pedigree suggests that 6f should pose no problems; looks well worth a go at this level and is no forlorn hope.
runners.comment: Powered clear late on to win on debut over 5f at Redcar, should get 6f, no forlorn hope

runners.name: Controlla
runners.spotlight: One of two for stable; Night Of Thunder filly; 160,000gns yearling; closely related to 8.6f AW 2yo/1m turf winner Khaloosy (RPR 114), half-sister to winners Moqtarreb (1m inc Listed; 109) and Zaajel (7f AW 2yo; 76); unplaced dam from family of Nathaniel; pitched in at the deep end on debut but yard's juveniles always respected.
runners.comment: 160,000gns Night Of Thunder filly, related to useful winners, pitched in at the deep end

runners.name: Maria Quiteria
runners.spotlight: Churchill filly justified skinny odds to win a five runner 5f Dundalk maiden decisively on debut in March; should stay 6f and handle quick ground on breeding but that form has taken some knocks and this demands a whole lot more.
runners.comment: Justified skinny odds on debut at Dundalk, should handle turf but this demands a lot more

runners.name: Dancing Destiny
runners.spotlight: Magna Grecia filly got off the mark on debut at Roscommon (7.5f, good) six days ago; didn't shape like she was crying out for a drop back in trip that day and her dam was best over 1m2f-1m4f; may be vulnerable to speedier rivals at this distance.
runners.comment: Debut win over 7.5f at Roscommon, not sure this drop back to 6f will suit

races.race_name: Goffs Lacken Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Havana Anna
runners.spotlight: Maiden and Listed winner over 5f; beaten only a nose in a Group 3 over the minimum trip at Longchamp, though produced her best effort when chasing home True Love in the 6f Group 1 Cheveley Park; failed to cope with the challenge of the Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf Sprint; appealing given her top-rating and she's unpenalised but lacks a run.
runners.comment: Listed winner/Group 1 placed is top-rated and unpenalised but lacks a run

runners.name: Charles Darwin
runners.spotlight: Beaten on debut at Cork before going the rest of the season unbeaten; landed a Navan maiden (6f, soft), a four-runner conditions event here (5f, good) and ultimately the Norfolk Stakes at Royal Ascot (5f, good to firm); workmanlike when justifying short odds at Navan on seasonal return (6f, good to yielding) and there is likely improvement to come; holds Group 1 entries for Ascot.
runners.comment: Last season's Norfolk Stakes winner was workmanlike on return but improvement likely

runners.name: Royal Bay Cen
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f French winner at two; good efforts in a pair of Group 3s before signing off her French career with an underwhelming Group 2 start; mightily impressive Irish/stable debut at Cork (5.5f, soft) when pulverising seven rivals by 9l; big chance if building on that on this better ground.
runners.comment: Mighty impressive Irish/stable debut in a Cork Listed race; big chance if handling ground

runners.name: Josh's Joy
runners.spotlight: Positive efforts in maidens before landing a Naas contest at the fourth attempt (6f, yielding); okay return in a Curragh handicap last time when a 4l seventh (6f, soft to heavy); this is a big ask.
runners.comment: Won her m'den at the fourth attempt last season; okay return but this is a big ask

runners.name: Oh Cecelia
runners.spotlight: Cork maiden winner (5f, good) for Jack Davison last year; made a winning stable debut in a Naas handicap in March (5f, soft) and added another victory in a Cork rated contest (5f, soft); beaten 9.5l by Royal Bay Cen in a Cork Listed race last time (5.5f, soft); a lot to find.
runners.comment: Beaten 9.5l by Royal Bay Cen in a Cork Listed race last time; a lot to find

runners.name: Kindergarten
runners.spotlight: Winner of her sole start last year at Navan (6f, yielding); disputing second in a Navan Listed contest before fading late into fourth (6f, good to yielding); plausible improvement to come from that first run back and was just 1.75l behind the winner Charles Darwin, although worse off at the weights now.
runners.comment: Plausible improvement to come over 1.75l defeat behind Charles Darwin on just second start

runners.name: Tahcawin
runners.spotlight: Easily won a 6f Fairyhouse nursery last season; built on a positive comeback against older rivals at Gowran to run a career best when filling the second spot in a Naas handicap eight days ago (6f, good); she's progressing, but looks out of her depth here.
runners.comment: Ran a career best last time when second in a h'cap but looks out of her depth here

runners.name: Chicago Call
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f Fairyhouse maiden over this trip last July (good); best form when just denied in a Listed event at the same track in September (6f, yielding); improved over moderate seasonal return at Cork when a 3l sixth at Navan last time (6f, good to yielding); others preferred.
runners.comment: Improved over moderate seasonal return when 3l behind at Navan latest; others preferred
runners.owner: Samuel M Mencoff & Anthony Paul Smurfit

races.race_name: Sole Power Sprint Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Mission Central
runners.spotlight: Won three of his six starts as a juvenile, including a Group 3 at the Curragh (6f, good to yielding) and a valuable conditions race at Ascot (6f, good); struggled when dropped to 5f at the Breeders' Cup on final start; ran out a ready winner of a C&D Listed contest on seasonal return to mark his first victory over the minimum trip; carries a penalty but big chance of following up.
runners.comment: Ready Listed C&D winner on seasonal return and big chance of following up under penalty

runners.name: Lady Iman
runners.spotlight: Amassed an impressive CV in her opening runs last season; won her AW maiden before landing a 5f Curragh Listed contest, and a Naas Group 3; failed to justify 8-15 odds in a Curragh Group 2 before resuming progress with a Molecomb Stakes victory at Goodwood; form tapered off in her final two Group runs; big player on ratings but lacks a run.
runners.comment: Impressive CV in opening runs last term; big player on ratings but lacks a run

runners.name: Likedbymike
runners.spotlight: 1-12 on turf but has had a few near misses, including when beaten a short head in a 5f Curragh handicap (soft) on penultimate; ran a career best over C&D last time when 0.75l behind Mission Central; better off with that rival now; good chance with a repeat.
runners.comment: 1-12 but some near misses inc' when 0.75l behind Mission Central here latest; chance
runners.owner: Mrs Michael J Byrne & Jerry P Murphy

runners.name: Kerdos
runners.spotlight: Classy sprinter for Clive Cox and won a Listed and a Group 2 for that yard; decent run on debut for this yard at Dundalk before disappointing in Cork Listed race next time on soft; much better over C&D last time when a 2.75l sixth behind Mission Central.
runners.comment: Listed and Gr.2 winner was a 2.75l sixth behind Mission Central last time over C&D
runners.owner: Michael Scaife/Liam Ferguson/Simon Murphy/H S Khunkhuna

runners.name: Keke
runners.spotlight: Seven wins over 5f and 6f generally on good ground; won off 97 in a 6f handicap here last time (good); his mark of 104 is a career high but he will need to improve again now entering stakes company.
runners.comment: Added seventh win when winning h'cap here last time; needs to improve again

runners.name: Valiant Force
runners.spotlight: Sole win on turf in the 2023 Norfolk Stakes; has won five conditions races at Dundalk since, the most recent a 4.25l win over 5f in February; well beaten at Lingfield, Newcastle and when 7.5l behind Keke in a 6f handicap here last time; others arrive in better form.
runners.comment: Just 1-12 on turf and 7.5l behind Keke in a h'cap last time; others arrive in better form
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Ipanema Queen
runners.spotlight: Winner of a 6f Curragh maiden but seemed better suited to 5f as the season progressed; won two Listed races at the Curragh and Dundalk over the minimum trip; narrowly lost out to longer priced stablemate on seasonal debut at Dundalk; 5-4 favourite at Cork (5.5f, soft) last time when a disappointing seventh of eight; this ground may suit better but others preferred.
runners.comment: Won a m'den and two Listed races last season but a disappointing seventh at Cork last time
runners.owner: Stonestreet Stables, LLC & AMO Racing Limited

runners.name: Gazelle d'Or
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner at 5f-6f on good and yielding ground; not at best on Curragh return; didn't get the clearest of runs when 12th of 15 and 5.5l behind the winner Mission Central in a C&D Listed contest last time; others preferred.
runners.comment: Not the clearest of runs when 12th of 15 in C&D Listed contest latest; others preferred

runners.name: Sarahmae
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner over this trip on varying ground types but hasn't been at her best so far this season and this looks too tough.
runners.comment: Three-time winner hasn't been at her best so far this season and this is tough
runners.owner: Mary B Hogan & Michael M O'Brien

runners.name: Billie Be Quick
runners.spotlight: Won her third handicap off 77 at Chepstow last August (5f, good to soft); no real impact in Listed races since and well beaten back in a handicap here last time (6f, good); others more appealing.
runners.comment: No real impact since winning h'cap off 77 last Aug'; others more appealing

runners.name: Black Forza
runners.spotlight: Winner of a Goodwood Group 2 in 2024; sparingly raced last year, well beaten in a Curragh Group 3 (6f, good); some better efforts twice at Dundalk but more needed here and lacks a run.
runners.comment: Gr.2 winner in 2024 ran okay on AW in final two runs; more needed here and lacks a run

runners.name: Moss Tucker
runners.spotlight: Former Flying Five winner reached a peak rating of 114 before losing his form in the latter half of 2024; missed all of last season; return in a C&D Listed contest last time was not totally devoid of promise when 4.75l behind Mission Central; may build on that.
runners.comment: Gr.1 winner in 2014 missed all of last season; some promise in his return here last time
